Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Hydrogenious LOHC Technologies GmbH develops, constructs, and operates infrastructure and systems for the storage, transport, and distribution of hydrogen using liquid organic hydrogen carrier (LOHC) technology. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Applied Carbon, Inc. designs and manufactures automated machines that convert agricultural crop waste into biochar for carbon sequestration and soil regeneration. Applied Carbon delivers carbon removal solutions by sequestering atmospheric carbon through biochar production and application, and provides agricultural services related to soil regeneration. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Diversified natural resources company, focused on coal, natural gas, copper, zinc, gold, silver and niobium mining and development.
Deal Resolution:
12 Jul 24 - Rio Tinto plc, the United Kingdom based international mining company with interests in mining for copper, gold, silver, coal, iron ore, molybdenum, titanium dioxide, uranium and diamonds, is reportedly interested in acquiring Teck Resources Ltd, the Canada based diversified natural resources company, focused on coal, natural gas, copper, zinc, gold, silver and niobium mining and development.

The market capitalization of Teck Resources Ltd is C$35.07bn ($25.74bn) based on the pre 1-day price of C$67.58 per share.

29 Jul 24 - Teck Resources Ltd attracts interest from Vale Base Metals and Anglo American.

08 Sep 25 - Anglo American plc, the listed United Kingdom based mining corporation that supplies bulk commodities including copper, iron ore, and diamonds primarily to industrial and manufacturing sectors & operates through integrated mining and processing facilities is to acquire Teck Resources Ltd, the listed Canada based diversified natural resources company, focused on coal, natural gas, copper, zinc, gold, silver and niobium mining and development, from a consortium led by Temagami Mining Co, the Canada based mining company providing extraction services of metalliferous ores. 

The consideration was CAD 27.93bn (US$20.2bn), based on the ratio of 1.3301 ordinary shares of Anglo American in exchange for each outstanding Teck Resources share, implying a stock offer price of CAD 56.5407 per share. 

The transaction values Teck Resources CAD 32.58bn (US$$23.56bn).

The combined company will be named Anglo Teck plc after completion & will be owned 62.4% by Anglo American shareholders & the remaining 37.6% by Teck Resources shareholders. 

The transaction is subject to approval by at least 66.67% shareholders of Teck Resources, court & regulatory approval, customary closing conditions, and is expected to close in 12-18 months.

10 Sep 25 - Teck Resources shareholder says higher price may be needed to seal Anglo American deal.

16 Sept 25 - Canadian Competition Bureau will review the transaction.

19 Sept 25 - The merger of copper miners Anglo American and Teck Resources is likely to garner a careful review by China's State Administration for Market Regulation (SAMR).

3 Oct 25 - Chilean competition authority FNE is expected to swiftly approve the proposed merger.

07 Oct 25 - Teck Resources has completed the Comprehensive operations review for the transaction.

06 Nov 25 - The transaction may receive a rival offer.

07 Nov 25 - Copper miners Anglo American and Teck Resources have lodged a merger filing with China's State Administration for Market Regulation.

10 Nov 25 - Shareholders' meeting for Anglo American will be held on 9 Dec 2025.

18 Nov 25 - The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has approved the transaction following a Phase I review.

19 Nov 25 - Canada’s Industry Minister has commented that the transaction is inadequate under the country’s industrial strategy.

26 Nov 25 - Proxy advisory firms Institutional Shareholder Services Inc and Glass Lewis & Co have recommended that Teck shareholders vote “FOR” the Company’s merger of equals with Anglo American plc.

27 Nov 25 - Canada’s federal government has cleared the acquisition.

08 Dec 25 - Anglo American has withdrawn Resolution 2 (amendment to executive long-term incentive awards) from the agenda of its 9 December 2025 shareholders’ meeting, while confirming that the merger remains conditional only on Resolution 1.

09 Dec 25 - The shareholders of Anglo American as well as Teck Resources have approved the transaction.

16 Dec 25 - Approval received from the Government of Canada.

6 Jan 25 - Anglo American and Teck Resources have filed their merger with the European Commission. The regulator has set a decision deadline for its review on 10 February. The merger was notified under the EC’s simplified procedure, a less burdensome process reserved for deals with low combined market shares.
Transaction Comments: Anglo American plc acquired a 1% stake in Teck Resources Ltd (100%) on September 08, 2025.

Mining companies faced operational constraints due to chronic power shortages, limiting their capacity.
There was a dramatic price appreciation in platinum group metals and gold, creating unprecedented valuations for domestic producers.
Smaller mining operations were under pressure to be acquired by larger producers due to scale economics.
International investment interest in South African assets was increasing, particularly from Asian and European investors.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: The merger was driven by commodity strength and operational synergies between Anglo American and Teck.
Improved infrastructure and reliable power supply allowed companies to evaluate acquisition targets based on full operational potential.
The significant price increases in platinum and gold made South African mining assets attractive for consolidation.
The deal aimed to capitalize on elevated commodity valuations and create a focused platform for further industry consolidation.
Cross-border capital flows and international interest in South African assets provided a conducive environment for the merger.

This reflects a strategic intent to consolidate resources and enhance market positioning amidst rising commodity prices.
Secondary Driver Justification: Operational synergies were a key factor in the merger, as noted in the reasoning that 'the merger was driven by commodity strength and operational synergies between Anglo American and Teck.' Improved infrastructure and reliable power supply also allowed for better evaluation of acquisition targets, suggesting a focus on enhancing operational efficiency.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Business Building: The text outlines a significant merger between Anglo American and Teck Resources aimed at creating a focused platform for further consolidation in the mining sector. This aligns with the intent of developing sustainable business models within the industry. **2. Logic: The rationale lies in the description of how the merger is driven by improved infrastructure and reliable power supply, which indicates a strategic move towards optimizing operations in a sustainable manner. The emphasis on capitalizing on elevated commodity valuations further supports the idea of creating sustainable growth opportunities in the sector. **3. Evidence: Phrases such as 'capitalizing on elevated commodity valuations' and 'operational synergies' indicate a focus on creating sustainable business growth, making this class the most relevant.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Valterra Platinum Limited engages in the production and supply of platinum group metals, base metals, and precious metals in South Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, and internationally. The company produces platinum, palladium, rhodium, ruthenium, iridium, and osmium; and nickel, copper, cobalt sulphate, and chrome, as well as gold and sodium sulphate. It also offers fuel cell electric vehicles. The company was formerly known as Anglo American Platinum Limited and changed its name to Valterra Platinum Limited in May 2025. The company was incorporated in 1946 and is headquartered in Johannesburg, South Africa.
Transaction Comments:
The Sellers have launched an accelerated book build offer of 52.2 million Placement Shares.

The Sellers successfully sold all of their stake in the Company and the book build was completed on September 4, 2025.

Settlement of the Placing Shares is expected to occur on or about September 9, 2025 on the Johannesburg Stock Exchange.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Anglo American was undergoing a strategic transformation, focusing on future-oriented commodities.
Platinum prices had more than doubled over the past year, while gold prices rose significantly.
Anglo American was divesting from non-core assets, including its remaining stake in Valterra Platinum.
The South African economy was experiencing slow growth, hampered by corruption and infrastructure issues.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: The divestment from Valterra Platinum allowed Anglo American to redirect capital towards more promising resources like copper and iron ore.
The deal was part of a broader trend of consolidation in the mining sector, driven by rising commodity prices and the need for scale.
Analysts viewed the sale as a strategic move to enhance Anglo American's focus on core assets and future growth opportunities.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal is part of a broader trend of consolidation in the mining sector, which is driven by rising commodity prices and the need for scale. This indicates that the primary intent behind the deal is to restructure the market by consolidating assets and focusing on core operations, as highlighted by Anglo American's divestment from non-core assets like Valterra Platinum.
Secondary Driver Justification: Valterra Platinum operates internationally across multiple regions, including South Africa, Asia, Europe, and North America. The divestment allows Anglo American to redirect capital towards more promising resources, which may include expanding their geographic footprint in these regions, thus indicating a secondary intent of geographic expansion.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Finance: The text discusses Valterra Platinum Limited's strategic transformation and divestment from non-core assets, highlighting a focus on investing in future-oriented commodities. This aligns with the definition of Sustainable Finance, which involves integrating environmental, social, and governance (ESG) impacts in financial decisions and mobilizing funds towards sustainable growth opportunities. 2. Evidence from the text: The decision by Anglo American to divest from Valterra Platinum and redirect capital towards more promising resources like copper and iron ore indicates a strategic financial maneuver aimed at achieving better long-term sustainability outcomes in a changing market.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Jellinbah Group Pty Ltd operates coal mines that produce metallurgical coal products. It offers hard coking coal, low volatile PCI coal, semi-soft coking coal, and thermal coal products. Jellinbah Group Pty Ltd was formerly known as Queensland Coal Mine Management Pty. Ltd. and changed its name to Jellinbah Group Pty Ltd in July 2009. The company was incorporated in 1987 and is based in Brisbane, Australia. As of January 29, 2025, Jellinbah Group Pty Ltd operates as a subsidiary of Zashvin Pty. Ltd.
Deal Resolution: Zashvin Pty. Ltd. completed the acquisition of an additional 33.30% stake in Jellinbah Group Pty Ltd from Anglo American plc (LSE:AAL) on January 29, 2025.
Transaction Comments:
Zashvin Pty. Ltd. agreed to acquire an additional 33.30% stake in Jellinbah Group Pty Ltd from Anglo American plc (LSE:AAL) for AUD 1.6 billion on November 4, 2024. A cash consideration of AUD 1.6 billion will be paid by Zashvin Pty. Ltd. As part of consideration, AUD 1.6 billion is paid towards common equity of Jellinbah Group Pty Ltd. Upon completion, Zashvin Pty. Ltd. will own 66.60% stake in Jellinbah Group Pty Ltd.

The transaction is subject to approval by regulatory board / committee and is expected to complete in the second quarter of 2025.

Morgan Stanley acted as the financial advisor to Anglo American. Corrs Chambers Westgarth acted as legal advisor to Zashvin Pty Ltd. The Corrs Chambers Westgarth was led by transaction partner Alexandra Feros and Zashvin relationship partner Joshua Paffey, and supported by partners James Morley, Franka Cheung, Cameron Blackwood, Jennee Chan, Anna White and Ian Reynolds. Sam Newhouse, Emily Cridland, Ed Barnett, Helen Lethaby, David Little and Christian McDermott of Latham & Watkins (London) LLP and Mark Morris, Jason Licht and Christopher Bezeg of Latham & Watkins LLP acted as legal advisors to Anglo American plc.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Anglo American was undergoing a significant restructuring of its executive team and portfolio.
The company had disposed of several assets, including its metallurgical coal assets in Australia.
Anglo American's regional director for Australia and Africa, Themba Mkhwanazi, was set to leave the company following these disposals.
Anglo was focusing on simplifying its portfolio to concentrate on copper, premium iron ore, and crop nutrients.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: The sale of the 33.3% stake in Jellinbah Group to Zashvin Ltd. was part of Anglo American's strategy to divest from non-core assets.
The deal was aligned with Anglo's goal of focusing on higher-margin and more valuable mining operations.
The transaction was expected to generate significant revenue, contributing to the overall financial health of the company.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal was part of Anglo American's strategy to divest from non-core assets, indicating a focus on restructuring its portfolio. This aligns with the broader trend of market consolidation as companies streamline operations and focus on core competencies, which is evident from Anglo's decision to simplify its portfolio by disposing of metallurgical coal assets.
Secondary Driver Justification: The transaction was expected to generate significant revenue, which suggests a focus on improving financial health through cost reduction. By divesting from lower-margin assets like Jellinbah Group, Anglo American can reduce operational costs and concentrate resources on higher-margin operations, thereby enhancing overall profitability.
rationale: 1. Energy Transition: The classification of 'Energy Transition' is appropriate as Jellinbah Group Pty Ltd operates coal mines, which are part of the fossil-based energy production system. Given that the text discusses Anglo American’s strategy to divest from non-core assets such as metallurgical coal, this highlights a shift away from fossil fuels towards potentially renewable energy sources. **2. Logic: The deal involving the sale of a stake in a coal mining company points to a transition in the energy sector where companies are adapting to the global environmental agenda of reducing reliance on coal. The evidence from the text including Anglo American's restructuring to focus on higher-margin and renewable assets signifies an industry trend towards energy transition.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Peace River Coal Inc. develops and operates coal mines and produces metallurgical coal. Peace River Coal Inc. was formerly known as Peace River Coal Limited Partnership and changed its name to Peace River Coal Inc. in 2011. The company was founded in 2006 and is based in Vancouver, Canada. As of February 11, 2025, Peace River Coal Inc. operates as a subsidiary of Conuma Resources Ltd.
Deal Resolution: Conuma Resources Ltd. completed the acquisition of Peace River Coal Inc. from Anglo American plc (LSE:AAL) on February 11, 2025.
Transaction Comments: Conuma Resources Ltd. agreed to acquire Peace River Coal Inc. from Anglo American plc (LSE:AAL) on September 20, 2024. In conjunction with the sale agreement, Anglo American will collaborate with Conuma Resources and Treaty 8 partners to maintain the Caribou Recovery Project, originally initiated by the Saulteau and West Moberly First Nations in 2012. As part of this initiative, Conuma Resources committed to relinquishing specific Peace River Coal tenures to safeguard caribou habitats and to implementing additional measures to support the recovery of the caribou population. The deal is subject to customary closing conditions including regulatory approvals.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Peace River Coal (PRC) was in care and maintenance mode since late 2014 due to low coal prices.
PRC faced significant environmental compliance issues, including exceeding selenium discharge limits and receiving a substantial fine.
The mine had not produced coal for several years, leading to operational stagnation.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Anglo American aimed to divest from underperforming assets and reduce its exposure to environmental liabilities.
Conuma Resources sought to expand its portfolio by acquiring high-quality steelmaking coal assets to enhance production capacity.
The deal was expected to facilitate a return to production at Peace River Coal under new ownership, benefiting local stakeholders and First Nations.
Primary Driver Justification: Conuma Resources sought to expand its portfolio by acquiring high-quality steelmaking coal assets, indicating a clear intent for portfolio diversification. This acquisition allows Conuma to enhance its production capacity, which aligns with their strategic goals in the coal market.
Secondary Driver Justification: The deal was expected to facilitate a return to production at Peace River Coal under new ownership, suggesting an operational efficiency improvement. By addressing the operational stagnation and environmental compliance issues, Conuma aims to revitalize the mine's productivity, benefiting local stakeholders.
rationale: 1. Hazardous substances: The text highlights significant environmental compliance issues faced by Peace River Coal Inc., particularly exceeding selenium discharge limits and incurring a substantial fine. This relates directly to the classification of hazardous substances as it involves the management and treatment of toxic pollutants. **2. Evidence: The phrase 'significant environmental compliance issues, including exceeding selenium discharge limits and receiving a substantial fine' demonstrates the company's challenges with hazardous substances, making this a relevant choice according to the definitions provided.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Valterra Platinum Limited engages in the production and supply of platinum group metals, base metals, and precious metals in South Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, and internationally. The company produces platinum, palladium, rhodium, ruthenium, iridium, and osmium; and nickel, copper, cobalt sulphate, and chrome, as well as gold and sodium sulphate. It also offers fuel cell electric vehicles. The company was formerly known as Anglo American Platinum Limited and changed its name to Valterra Platinum Limited in May 2025. The company was incorporated in 1946 and is headquartered in Johannesburg, South Africa.
Deal Resolution: Anglo American plc (LSE:AAL) plc completed the spin-off of 51% stake in Valterra Platinum Limited (JSE:VAL) on May 31, 2025. The demerger took effect on May 31, 2025 with the share consolidation becoming effective on June 1, 2025. Following the demerger Anglo American will continue to hold c.19.9% of Valterra Platinum and intends to retain this shareholding for at least 90 days following the demerger (subject to appropriate exceptions for any sale to maintain, and not exceed, this c.19.9% interest), with this shareholding position to be managed responsibly over time to effect a full separation. Following the demerger, Valterra shares were admitted to trading on the London Stock Exchange’s Main Market and to listing in the Equity Shares (International Commercial Companies Secondary Listing) category. Its primary listing remains the Johannesburg Stock Exchange’s Main Board. Harry Coghill, Max Mittasch and Mark Slade of The Macfarlanes acted as legal advisor to Anglo American. Fiona Edmundson of Bowman Gilfillan acted as legal advisor to Anglo American. Linklaters LLP acted as legal advisor to Anglo American plc.
Transaction Comments:
Anglo American plc (LSE:AAL) agreed to spin-off 51% stake in Anglo American Platinum Limited (JSE:AMS) for ZAR 97.5 billion on May 14, 2024. Upon completion, Anglo American Platinum Ltd., would become an independent entity held by existing Anglo American shareholders. Anglo American intends to retain a 19.9% shareholding in Anglo American Platinum. As part of the transaction, shareholders will receive 110 AMS shares for every 1075 Anglo American shares held.

The transaction is subject to regulatory approvals from South African regulators and approval of the Demerger Distribution by the Anglo American Board. As of September 2, 2024, The Company has been informed by Anglo American that certain Anglo American subsidiaries in South Africa have undertaken an internal re-organization in preparation for the Anglo American Platinum Demerger. The resulting changes are administrative in nature and have no impact on Anglo American's beneficial interest in the Company. As of February 17, 2025, Anglo American intends to retain a 19.9% shareholding in Anglo American Platinum. Anglo American Platinum has announced its final dividend for 2024, and an additional cash dividend, together totaling ZAR 16.5 billion, which will be payable to all Anglo American Platinum shareholders ahead of the demerger. The shareholders meeting is scheduled to be held on April 30, 2025. On February 17, 2025, the company confirmed its plans to finalize the demerger by June. As of March 17, 2025, Nolitha Fakude, Themba Mkhwanazi, and Matt Daley have resigned from their positions as non-executive directors. Anglo American Platinum has proposed changing its name to Valterra Platinum ahead of its imminent demerger from the Anglo American group. As of April 8, 2025, Anglo American has published the circular to seek the approval of its shareholders for the demerger at a General Meeting to be held on Wednesday, April 30, 2025. As of April 30, 2025, Anglo American plc shareholders approved the deal. The deal is now expected to complete on May 31, 2025. As on May 8, 2025, Anglo American Plc has announced a strategic move involving the demerger and renaming of its Anglo American Platinum unit, a decision that was formalized at a shareholder meeting held on April 30, 2025. This development is set to reshape the company's structure and market presence, as shareholders prepare for the changes slated to take effect by June 2, 2025. As of May 20, 2025, the effective date for the demerger will be Saturday, 31 May 2025, and on Monday, 2 June 2025, AAP's ordinary shares will be admitted to the equity shares (international commercial companies secondary listing) listing segment of the FCA's Official List and admitted to trading on the London Stock Exchange's ("LSE") Main Market for listed securities, which will be in addition to AAP's existing primary listing on the Johannesburg Stock Exchange ("JSE"). effective date for Anglo American's share consolidation is Sunday, June 1, 2025 and unconditional dealings in the consolidated shares (with new ISIN GB00BTK05J60 and SEDOL: BTK05J6) will commence on the LSE on Monday, June 2, 2025, with the listing and commencement of dealings in entitlements to the consolidated shares on the JSE also on Monday, June 2, 2025. Centerview Partners has acted as a financial advisor to Anglo American.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Anglo American was undergoing a strategic transformation, including a merger with Teck Resources.
Platinum prices had more than doubled, while gold prices had risen significantly, indicating a commodities boom.
Valterra Platinum was spun off from Anglo American as part of a portfolio optimization strategy.
The South African economy had been growing at less than 1% annually for a decade, affected by corruption and infrastructure issues.

The spinoff of Valterra Platinum allowed Anglo American to redirect capital towards more promising ventures.

This restructuring aims to consolidate its operations around more lucrative commodities, which is further supported by the favorable environment created by rising commodity prices.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Operations: The text describes Valterra Platinum Limited's focus on the production and supply of various metals, including platinum and palladium, which are essential in the transition to renewable energy technologies such as fuel cells. This aligns with the concept of 'Sustainable Operations' as it implies efforts to decarbonize and optimize manufacturing processes while embedding sustainability metrics into operations. 2. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Minas-Rio of Vale S.A. comprises iron ore producing property. The asset is located in Brazil.
Deal Resolution: Vale S.A. (BOVESPA:VALE3)completed the acquisition of 15% stake in Minas-Rio of Vale S.A. from Anglo American plc (LSE:AAL) on December 2, 2024. Anglo American will continue to control, manage and operate Minas-Rio, including any future expansion.
Transaction Comments: Vale S.A. (BOVESPA:VALE3) agreed to acquire 15% stake in Minas-Rio of Vale S.A. from Anglo American plc (LSE:AAL) on February 22, 2024. Under the Transaction’s terms, Vale will contribute Serpentina and $157.5 million in cash to acquire a 15% shareholding in the enlarged Minas-Rio, subject to normal completion adjustments. Vale will also have an option to acquire an additional 15% shareholding in the enlarged Minas-Rio for cash (at fair value calculated at the time of exercise of the option), if and when certain events relating to a future expansion occur. Additionally, depending on the future iron ore prices, there may be an adjustment in the transaction price and the fair value adjustments of this mechanism will be recognized in the Company's income statements accordingly. Upon completion of the transaction, Anglo American Brasil will be an associate of Vale and the investment will be accounted for under the equity method. The combination of Minas-Rio with the scale and quality of the Serpentina endowment also offers considerable expansion opportunities, including the potential to double production towards 60Mtpa. The Transaction is subject to regulatory conditions and is expected to complete in Q4 2024. Rory O'Halloran, Lara Aryani, Cynthia Urda Kassis, Maegen Morrison and Nick Withers of Shearman & Sterling LLP acted as legal advisor to Anglo American plc (LSE:AAL). Morgan Stanley acted as the financial advisor to Anglo American.
Primary Driver Justification: The acquisition of a 15% stake in Minas-Rio of Vale S.A. allows Vale S.A. to expand its geographic footprint in Brazil, a key region for iron ore production. This strategic move enhances Vale's presence in a significant mining area, aligning with its broader geographic expansion goals.
Secondary Driver Justification: By acquiring a stake in Minas-Rio, Vale S.A. is also positioning itself to access valuable iron ore resources. This resource acquisition is crucial for maintaining and potentially increasing its production capabilities in the competitive mining sector.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Operations: The acquisition of a stake in Minas-Rio of Vale S.A. indicates a strategic decision aimed at improving operational sustainability. Vale S.A. aims to optimize its manufacturing and production systems in iron ore extraction. 2. LOGIC: The focus on improving mining operations often encompasses methodologies that aim to reduce emissions, resource usage, and increase efficiency in extracting minerals while maintaining environmental stewardship. 3. EVIDENCE: The text mentions Vale's involvement in managing and operating the Minas-Rio property, which implies efforts toward operational efficiency and sustainability in resource management.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Decarbonization company that designs, manufactures, and distributes hybrid battery systems and hydrogen fuel cell technologies.
Deal Resolution:
Cummins Inc, the listed United States based that designs and manufactures diesel and alternative fuel engines and generators, and related components and technology is to acquire First Mode Holdings Inc, the local decarbonization company that designs, manufactures, and distributes hybrid battery systems and hydrogen fuel cell technologies in a bankruptcy deal.  The consideration was USD 15m.

The transaction is subject to bankruptcy court approval.

11 Feb 25 - The transaction was completed.
Transaction Comments: Cummins Inc acquired a 1% stake in First Mode Holdings Inc (100%) on December 15, 2024.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Anglo American was unable to find industry partners to support its initiative to replace diesel trucks with hydrogen-fueled vehicles.
The company had invested up to $70 million in developing climate-friendly mining technology.
Anglo American faced pressure from investors to reduce carbon emissions due to concerns about global warming.
The company had developed a prototype hydrogen-powered truck capable of carrying significant ore loads while reducing emissions.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Acquiring First Mode Holdings will enhance Anglo American's capabilities in carbon reduction technologies.
The deal aligns with Anglo American's goal of becoming carbon neutral by 2040.
The partnership will support the development and scaling of hydrogen-fueled vehicles for mining operations.
First Mode's expertise will assist in reducing the costs associated with converting diesel trucks to hydrogen.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal aligns with Anglo American's goal of becoming carbon neutral by 2040, indicating a strong focus on sustainability. Additionally, acquiring First Mode Holdings will enhance their capabilities in carbon reduction technologies, which is crucial for meeting investor pressures to reduce carbon emissions due to global warming.
Secondary Driver Justification: First Mode Holdings specializes in hybrid battery systems and hydrogen fuel cell technologies, which represents a significant technological advancement for Anglo American. The partnership will support the development and scaling of hydrogen-fueled vehicles for mining operations, showcasing a clear intent to innovate and improve their technological offerings.
rationale: 1. Energy Transition: The text discusses the acquisition of First Mode Holdings, a company that focuses on hybrid battery systems and hydrogen fuel technologies, to help Anglo American replace diesel trucks with hydrogen-fueled vehicles, which is directly aligned with the shift from fossil-fuel-based energy systems to renewable energy sources. 2. Evidence: The statement "Acquiring First Mode Holdings will enhance Anglo American's capabilities in carbon reduction technologies" and the goal of becoming carbon neutral by 2040 further substantiate this classification, indicating a commitment to energy transition.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Comprises two mines in Brazil
Deal Resolution:
14 May 24 - Anglo American has announced a plan to unlock significant value from its portfolio and accelerate the delivery of consistently stronger shareholder returns.

The plan includes a divestment of its nickel business.

24 Jan 25 - Bids from interested bidders have been collected including China Minmetals Corporation and International Resources Holding.

18 Feb 25 - MMG Ltd, the Australia based copper and zinc mining company through its subsidiary MMG Singapore Resources Pte Ltd is to acquire Anglo American N'iquel Brasil Ltda, the Brazil based company that comprises of two mines in Brazil from Anglo American plc, the United Kingdom based mining corporation engaged in exploration and production of iron ore, platinum group metals, copper and diamond. The consideration was $500m, which consist of $350m paid in cash, $150m paid in earnout.

The Transaction is subject to a number of conditions, including customary competition and regulatory clearances completion is expected by Q3 2025.

26 Aug 25 - The General Superintendent’s Office (SG) of Brazil’s competition authority CADE has opened a gun-jumping investigation.

12 Sept 25 - MMG Ltd expected that the European Union competition regulators will approve the transaction. 

17 Sep 25 - The General Superintendent’s Office (SG) of Brazil’s CADE has dismissed gun-jumping investigation.

15 Oct 25 - MMG has submitted a remedy offer to the European Commission (EC) in a bid to gain Phase I approval. MMG has committed to supply Anglo American with an agreed amount of ferronickel from the two Brazilian mines. 

04 Nov 25 - The European Commission has opened an in-depth investigation in relation to the transaction.
Transaction Comments: MMG Ltd acquired a 1% stake in Anglo American N'iquel Brasil Ltda (100%) on February 18, 2025.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal involves the divestment of Anglo American's nickel business, indicating a strategic move to consolidate its operations and focus on core assets. This aligns with the goal of unlocking significant value from its portfolio, suggesting a restructuring effort to enhance market positioning and efficiency in the mining sector.
Secondary Driver Justification: The transaction is expected to accelerate the delivery of stronger shareholder returns, which often involves reducing costs associated with underperforming assets. By divesting the nickel business, Anglo American can streamline operations and potentially lower operational costs, contributing to improved financial performance.
rationale: 1. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Diamantina Copper-Gold Project in western Queensland.
Deal Resolution:
Strategic Energy Resources Ltd, the Australia based exploration company for oil and gas, has entered into an agreement to acquire 100% of Diamantina Copper-Gold Project in western Queensland from Anglo American plc, the United Kingdom based mining corporation supplies bulk commodities including copper, iron ore, and diamonds primarily to industrial and manufacturing sectors. The consideration was 0.51m AUD ($0.33m), which consist of 0.3m AUD paid in cash, 0.21m AUD paid in common shares. 30,000,000 consideration shares will be issued, valued at 0.21m AUD based on Strategic Energy Resources Ltd's closing share price of 0.007 AUD per share on one day prior to announcement date. Completion is subject to Ministerial approval.

6 Nov 25 - Deal completed. The consideration was 0.51m AUD ($0.33m), which consist of 0.3m AUD paid in cash, 0.21m AUD paid in common shares. 30,000,000 consideration shares will be issued, valued at 0.21m AUD based on Strategic Energy Resources Ltd's closing share price of 0.007 AUD per share on the completion date.
Transaction Comments: Strategic Energy Resources Ltd acquired a 1% stake in Mining Assets (Diamantina Copper-Gold Project, 100%) on July 18, 2025.

The merger could have potentially addressed the increasing demand for metals essential for clean energy systems.

This suggests a strategic intent to strengthen positions within a consolidating market, which is a key aspect of market consolidation and restructuring.

The deal aligns with the increasing investor interest in mining assets, particularly in the context of growing demand for metals essential for clean energy systems.
rationale: 1. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: I-Pulse Inc. develops and manufactures pulsed power-based technologies for the natural resources, global energy, manufacturing, and the research and development industries. The company's products include I-ROX, a pulsed power technology solution for breaking rocks in mines; i-mine, a continuous and scalable mining and minerals processing solution for rock faces; IPW Typhoon, a geophysical survey technology product to locate hidden water resources underground; G-Pulse, a drilling technology solution for the geothermal energy sector; Blue Spark Geothermal, a blockage clearing product for geothermal wells; and Bmax, a metal forming and welding solution for advanced manufacturing applications. I-Pulse Inc. was incorporated in 2007 and is based in Wilmington, Delaware with additional offices in Rochester Hills, Michigan; Tokyo, Japan; Toulouse, France; Vancouver, Canada, and London, United Kingdom.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Boston Electrometallurgical Corporation manufactures and commercializes steel through molten oxide electrolysis (MOE) technology. The company's technology utilizes an electrolytic cell to process raw iron ore and produce ferroalloys that are used to produce certain grades of steel. Its MOE technology is also used to reclaim valuable metals from waste produced during the mining process. The company was incorporated in 2012 and is based in Woburn, Massachusetts with an additional office in Coronel Xavier Chaves, Brazil and a warehouse facility in Woburn, Massachusetts.
Buyers/Investors: BHP Group Limited (ASX:BHP); ArcelorMittal S.A. (ENXTAM:MT); M&G Investment Management Limited; Baillie Gifford & Co Ltd.; BMW i Ventures, Inc.; M12; Prelude Ventures, LLC; Breakthrough Energy Ventures, LLC; Aramco Ventures; Siteground Capital Ltd

The transaction included participation from new investors Prosperity7 Ventures, M&G Investment Management Limited, Goehring & Rozencwajg Resources Fund, Baillie Gifford & Co Ltd., Climate Innovation Fund, a fund managed by M12, returning investors Breakthrough Energy LLC, BHP Group Limited, Prelude Ventures, LLC, and several others made additional investments in the transaction. Transaction Comments:
CRH plc (NYSE:CRH) intent to acquire 57.3% stake in Adbri Limited (ASX:ABC) for AUD1.1 billion on December 17, 2023. CRH plc (NYSE:CRH) agreed to acquire 57.3% stake in Adbri Limited (ASX:ABC) from UBS Group AG (SWX:UBSG) and others for AUD1.1 billion on February 26, 2024. If the Scheme is approved and implemented, each Independent Adbri Shareholder will be entitled to receive AUD 3.20 for each Scheme Share and The maximum consideration payable by CRH in connection with the Scheme will be AUD 1,206,308,768 (based on the maximum number of Adbri Shares permitted to be on issue on the Scheme Record Date under the SID). Subject to agreement of a binding Scheme Implementation Agreement on terms acceptable to the parties, the intention of the Independent Board Committee is to unanimously recommend the proposal, in the absence of a superior proposal and subject to an independent expert concluding (and continuing to conclude) that the scheme of arrangement is fair and reasonable and in the best interests of Adbri shareholders. CRH proposes to fully fund the Scheme using existing cash reserves of the CRH Group. As per the transaction, a break fee, or reverse break fee, each of approximately AUD 21 million may be payable by Adbri or CRH. The Proposal is subject to a number of conditions including no material adverse changes to the business; CRH completing satisfactory due diligence; Foreign Investment Review Board (“FIRB”) approval; and the Adbri independent directors unanimously recommending and committing to vote in favour of the Proposal. As of May 3, 2024, The Supreme Court of New South Wales made the following orders in relation to the Scheme that Adbri convenes and holds a meeting of Independent Adbri Shareholders to consider and vote on the Scheme (Scheme Meeting); and approving the distribution of the scheme booklet providing information about the Scheme and a notice of Scheme Meeting (Scheme Booklet) to Independent Adbri Shareholders. CRH’s total long position is 309,419,488 Adbri Shares, representing 47.3% of Adbri Shares as at the Last Practicable Date. The Scheme Meeting will be held on June 12, 2024 and Court hearing to approve the Scheme will on June 14, 2024. The transaction is expected to become effective on June 17, 2024. As of May 31, 2024, Adbri is pleased to announce that CRH has received written confirmation from the Foreign Investment Review Board (FIRB) that the Commonwealth Government has no objection under the Foreign Acquisitions and Takeovers Act 1975 to the acquisition by CRH of the Scheme Shares. As of June 6, 2024, the Scheme meeting subject to Adbri Shareholders approving the Scheme to be held on June 12, 2024; and the Court hearing to approve the Scheme will take place on June 14, 2024. As of June 12, 2024, independent Adbri Shareholders vote in favour of Scheme of Arrangement. As of June 14, 2024, the Supreme Court of New South Wales has ordered approving the proposed acquisition by CRH ANZ Pty Ltd of all the issued shares in Adbri that Barro Group Pty Ltd and its associates (Barro Group) do not own, by way of a scheme. Adbri to lodge an office copy of the orders of the Court with the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) on June 17, 2024, at which time the Scheme will become effective. Subject to the Scheme becoming legally effective, Independent Adbri Shareholders will be sent AUD 3.20 cash per Adbri Share on the Implementation Date for the Scheme, expected to be July 1, 2024.

Adbri has engaged J.P. Morgan and Barrenjoey as its financial advisers and Rebecca Maslen-Stannage and Mia Harrison-Kelf of Herbert Smith Freehills as its legal adviser. Adbri Independent Directors appointed Grant Samuel as the Independent Expert, concluded that the Scheme is fair and reasonable and will receive a fixed fee of AUD 600,000 for the preparation of this report.. PricewaterhouseCoopers as tax advisor to Adbri in relation to the Scheme, and Computershare as the Adbri Share Registry. Macquarie Capital (Australia) Limited acted as financial advisor to Barro Group Pty Ltd.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: As of January 4, 2024, Lime Operations Of CRH In Europe was acquired by SigmaRoc plc. Lime Operations Of CRH In Europe represents the combined operations of Clogrennane Lime Limited, Fels Holding Gmbh, and Vapenka Vitosov S.R.O. in their sale to SigmaRoc plc. Clogrennane Lime Limited produces and distributes chemical lime. Clogrennane Lime Limited is based in Ireland. Fels Holding Gmbh is based in Germany. Vapenka Vitosov S.R.O. is based in Czech Republic.
Deal Resolution: SigmaRoc plc (AIM:SRC) completed the acquisition of Lime Operations Of CRH In Europe from CRH plc (NYSE:CRH) on January 4, 2024. As of March 27, 2024, SigmaRoc completed the acquisition of CRH's lime operations in UK. Nigel Cooke of Eversheds Sutherland acted as legal advisor to CRH plc.
Target/Issuer: Lime Operations Of CRH In Europe
Transaction Comments:
SigmaRoc plc (AIM:SRC) agreed to acquire Lime Operations Of CRH In Europe from CRH plc (NYSE:CRH) for approximately €750 million on November 22, 2023. The deal consideration is €745 million (including approximately €211.5 million in connection with the assignment of the German Intercompany Loan Receivables) (subject to customary adjustments in respect of the target entities' net debt and working capital position as at January 1, 2024). In addition, the Company has entered into the Call Options pursuant to which, subject to certain conditions, it has been granted the right (but not the obligation) to acquire, separately, the Deal 2 Target (the UK Target) and the Deal 3 Target (the Polish Target), being the UK and Polish lime operations of CRH. The Business comprises 16 operating locations with leading market positions across Ireland, the UK, Germany, Czech Republic and Poland. The combined Business generated sales of c.$610 million (€557.902 million) and EBITDA of c.$137 million (€125.299 million) in 2022. Upfront consideration of €825 million and €175 million deferred consideration financed through a combination of €505 million from the New Facilities, €230 million ordinary equity raise and purchase price adjustments. As of December 11, 2023, shareholders of CRH plc have approved the transaction. The transaction is structured in three phases, the first of which is expected to complete in early 2024 comprising the Group's lime operations at home and in Germany, Czech Republic and Ireland. As of January 4, 2024, SigmaRoc completed the acquisition of CRH's lime operations in Germany, Czech Republic and Ireland. The remaining phases, consisting of operations in the UK and Poland, are expected to complete in 2024. As of March 27, 2024, SigmaRoc completed the acquisition of CRH's lime operations in UK.

Dru Danford, Ben Cryer, Mark Harrison, John More, Anake Singh, Oliver Baxendale, Oliver Houghton, Will Game, Joel Knight, Chris Cooper, Paras Amlani, Charlie Campbell and Edward Prest of Liberum Capital Limited, BNP Paribas SA, Banco Santander, S.A. and Rothschild & Co SCA acted as financial advisor to SigmaRoc plc (AIM:SRC). David Lewis, Mark Richardson, Ingrid Wijnmalen, Philip Broke, Marc Israel, James Johnson, Helen Joseph, Will Smith, Philip Trillmich, Bodo Bender, Marek Sawicki, Jonathan Weinberg, Alisa Preissler, Thyl Hassler, Jakub Gubanski, Tallat Hussain, of White & Case LLP acted as legal advisor to SigmaRoc plc on the acquisition. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Horizon Organic Dairy, LLC./Wallaby Yogurt Company, Inc. represents the combined operation Horizon Organic Dairy, LLC. and Wallaby Yogurt Company, Inc. in their sale to Platinum Equity, LLC. Wallaby Yogurt Company, Inc. produces and supplies organic yogurts. The company is based in USA.
Deal Resolution: Platinum Equity, LLC completed the acquisition of an unknown majority stake in Horizon Organic and Wallaby from Danone S.A. (ENXTPA:BN) on April 2, 2024.
Pre-Deal Situation: Danone S.A. (ENXTPA:BN) announced it will explore strategic options, including a potential sale, for its organic dairy activity in the U.S., comprised of the Horizon Organic and Wallaby businesses. This announcement is an additional step in the portfolio review and asset rotation program the company announced in March 2022, as part of the Renew Danone strategy. Horizon Organic and Wallaby are strong brands in the U.S. organic dairy category, with a portfolio of organic dairy products, including milk, creamers & whiteners, yogurt, cheese, and butter. They represent approximately 3% of Danone’s global revenues and had a dilutive impact on Danone’s like-for-like sales growth and recurring operating margin in 2022. Antoine de Saint-Affrique, Chief Executive Officer, said: “Both Horizon Organic and Wallaby are strong, much-loved brands with compelling growth opportunities. That said, seen through the lens of our Renew Strategy which requires us to stay disciplined in how we allocate our resources, they fall outside our priority growth areas of focus. We are confident that exploring strategic options for these brands will enable them to get the focus and resources they need and therefore allow them to maximize their potential and unlock further growth".
Target/Issuer: Horizon Organic Dairy, LLC./Wallaby Yogurt Company, Inc.
Transaction Comments: Platinum Equity, LLC signed a definitive agreement to acquire an unknown majority stake in Horizon Organic and Wallaby from Danone S.A. (ENXTPA:BN) on January 1, 2024. The proposed acquisition of Horizon Organic and Wallaby is subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approval. Morgan Lewis is serving as legal advisor and Alston & Bird is serving as debt financing counsel to Platinum Equity. Centerview Partners LLC acted as financial advisor to Danone S.A.  Nicolas Guillo, Firas Abou Merhi and team of Mazars Group, Investment Banking Arm acted as due diligence provider to Danone in this transaction.

ProViva was produced at a facility in Lunnarp, Sweden, which was Danone's first production facility in Sweden since 2010.
There was a growing trend among Nordic consumers towards plant-rich diets and increased awareness of environmental issues.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Danone aimed to divest ProViva to focus on its core business and strengthen its portfolio in plant-based products.
The sale to Lactalis allowed Danone to streamline operations and potentially enhance its sustainability efforts.
The deal aligns with the increasing consumer demand for healthier and more sustainable food options in the Nordic market.
Primary Driver Justification: Danone aimed to divest ProViva to focus on its core business and strengthen its portfolio in plant-based products, indicating a strategic move towards portfolio diversification. This aligns with the growing trend among Nordic consumers towards plant-rich diets, suggesting a shift in Danone's product offerings to better match consumer preferences.
Secondary Driver Justification: The sale to Lactalis allowed Danone to streamline operations and potentially enhance its sustainability efforts, which indicates a focus on ESG / sustainability alignment. Additionally, the deal aligns with the increasing consumer demand for healthier and more sustainable food options in the Nordic market, further emphasizing the importance of sustainability in this transaction.
rationale: 1. Customer health and wellness: The text discusses a nutritional supplement provider that offers products aimed at enhancing health and wellness, such as adrenal support, probiotics, and hormone balance. These products are specifically labeled as pharmaceutical-grade, indicating a focus on high-quality health products. **2. Evidence: The company's operations are centered on providing accessible, science-backed wellness solutions to consumers via subscription services, reflecting a commitment to customer health and well-being. Furthermore, the overall trend is shifting towards healthier and sustainable food options, as indicated by consumer demands in the Nordic market.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited produces and sells electrolytic aluminum and alumina in China. The company engages in alumina refining and aluminum smelting. Its electrolytic aluminum products primarily include liquid aluminum and aluminum ingots. The company is also involved in wind and photovoltaic power generation and production activities. It serves the 3C electronics, lightweight automotive, green energy, transportation, industrial materials, and construction industries. The company was founded in 2012 and is based in Tongliao, China. Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited is a subsidiary of Bloomsbury Holding Limited.

Pre_Deal_Situation: Chuangxin Industries was preparing for an IPO in Hong Kong, reflecting a growing interest in the aluminum sector.
The company had a mixed financial performance, with increased revenues but declining net profits due to investments in future growth.
Chuangxin Industries was strategically positioned in a favorable market for aluminum, coinciding with rising commodity prices.
The company had established a strong presence in resource-rich regions and maintained high self-sufficiency in alumina and electricity.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: The IPO raised significant capital (approximately $707 million) to fund expansion and green energy projects.
Glencore's participation as a cornerstone investor indicates confidence in Chuangxin's business model and growth potential.
The deal aligns with the broader trend of increasing demand for aluminum driven by construction and technology sectors.
Chuangxin's integrated business model and focus on sustainability positioned it favorably in the evolving aluminum market.
Primary Driver Justification: The IPO raised significant capital (approximately $707 million) to fund expansion and green energy projects, indicating a strong focus on growth in high-demand sectors such as construction and technology. Additionally, the deal aligns with the broader trend of increasing demand for aluminum, which is driven by these sectors, showcasing Chuangxin's strategic positioning for growth exposure.
Secondary Driver Justification: Chuangxin's integrated business model and focus on sustainability positioned it favorably in the evolving aluminum market. The emphasis on funding green energy projects reflects a commitment to ESG principles, which is increasingly important in attracting investment and aligning with market trends.
rationale: 1. Energy Transition: The text highlights Chuangxin Industries' involvement in wind and photovoltaic power generation, which is crucial for the shift to renewable energy sources away from fossil fuels. This aligns with the concept of Energy Transition. 2. Evidence: The company's focus on expanding green energy projects as part of its IPO strategy demonstrates an intentional move towards sustainable energy practices, essential in the context of current global energy trends.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Li-Cycle Holdings Corp. operates as a lithium-ion battery resource recovery company in North America. The company processes battery manufacturing scrap and end-of-life batteries to produce black mass, a powder-like substance, which contains lithium, nickel and cobalt, and other intermediate products; and critical battery-grade materials for the lithium-ion battery supply chain, including lithium carbonate, as well as black mass and products analogous to black mass that have a similar metal content and shredded copper and aluminum material. It also offers services for recycling lithium-ion batteries, which include coordination of logistics and destruction of batteries. The company serves consumer electronics, electric vehicles, and battery energy storage systems industries. Li-Cycle Holdings Corp. was founded in 2016 and is headquartered in Toronto, Canada. As of August 7, 2025, Li-Cycle Holdings Corp. operates as a subsidiary of Glencore Canada Corporation.
Deal Resolution: On March 25, 2024, Li-Cycle Holdings Corp closed the transaction.
Transaction Comments:
Li-Cycle Holdings Corp. announced that it has entered into a note purchase agreement with returning investor Glencore plc and new investor, Glencore Canada Corporation to issue a senior secured convertible note in an aggregate principal amount of $75 million on March 11, 2024. The interest on a semi-annual basis from the date of issuance, and will be based on the secured overnight financing rate plus five percent (5%) per annum if interest is paid in cash and plus six percent (6%) per annum if interest is paid in PIK. In the case that an event of default has occurred and is continuing, the interest rate will be the rate stated above, plus one percent (1%) per annum (which additional 1% will be payable in cash). The principal and accrued interest owing under the Senior Secured Convertible Note may be converted at any time by the holder into common shares in the capital of the Company at a conversion price of $0.53 per share. The Noteholder shall not offer, sell, assign or transfer (including through hedging or derivative transactions) any Common Shares issued upon conversion of the Note or exercise of any of the Redemption Warrants other than to a Permitted Transferee for a period of twelve (12) months from the Closing Date.

Andrea M. Basham and Allison R. Liff of Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer US LLP acted as legal advisors to company. Jonathan Grant and Fraser Bourne of McCarthy Tétrault LLP are acting as legal advisor to the company. David Avery Gee, Heather Emmel and Nitin Konchady of Weil, Gotshal & Manges LLP are acting as legal advisor to Glencore Canada Corporation.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Li-Cycle was in the final phase of liquidation, with its operational business already under new management.
An insolvency administrator was managing the remnants of what was once a significant growth story.
The company faced bankruptcy due to an inability to secure funds for building its recycling hub in Rochester, New York.
The battery recycling sector was experiencing a downturn, exacerbated by falling lithium prices and reduced demand for certain battery materials.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Glencore acquired Li-Cycle to gain access to its proprietary battery recycling technology.
The acquisition allowed Glencore to expand its portfolio in the battery materials sector amidst a market shift towards energy storage solutions.
By acquiring Li-Cycle, Glencore aimed to strengthen its position in a consolidating battery industry and leverage Li-Cycle's existing capabilities.
Primary Driver Justification: Glencore acquired Li-Cycle to gain access to its proprietary battery recycling technology, which indicates a clear intent to enhance its technological capabilities in the battery materials sector. This acquisition is crucial for Glencore as it seeks to innovate and improve its offerings in a rapidly evolving market focused on energy storage solutions.
Secondary Driver Justification: The acquisition allowed Glencore to strengthen its position in a consolidating battery industry, suggesting a strategic move to enhance market share and competitive positioning. Given the context of Li-Cycle's pre-deal situation, where it was facing bankruptcy and operational challenges, this deal also reflects a restructuring effort within the battery recycling sector.
rationale: Serial No. Material use, waste & circularity: The text discusses Li-Cycle's operations in processing and recycling lithium-ion batteries to recover valuable materials like lithium, nickel, and cobalt, which aligns with responsible sourcing and recycling practices. Logic: The focus is on recycling end-of-life batteries and the production of intermediate products, reinforcing the concept of circular economy. Evidence: The description mentions the processing of battery scrap into 'black mass' and the sale of battery-grade materials, highlighting efforts in reducing waste and promoting material reuse.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: GT Resources Inc. engages in the exploration and development of mineral resource properties in Canada and Finland. It primarily explores for nickel, copper, cobalt, platinum group elements, gold, palladium, and zinc deposits. The company was formerly known as Palladium One Mining Inc. and changed its name to GT Resources Inc. in March 2024. GT Resources Inc. is headquartered in Toronto, Canada.
Deal Resolution: On May 15, 2023, Palladium One Mining Inc. (TSXV:PDM) closed the transaction.
Transaction Comments: Palladium One Mining Inc. announced a private placement of 7,439,071 common shares at a price of CAD 0.11 per share for a gross proceeds of CAD 818,297.80 on May 15, 2023. The transaction included participation from returning investor, Glencore plc for 9.99% stake. All common shares issued are subject to a four-month hold period from the closing date. No fees were paid in connection with the transaction.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Palladium demand was experiencing a decline due to a drop in global auto sales and production.
The automotive sector, which accounted for over 80% of palladium demand, was facing pressure from the growing market for electric vehicles (EVs).
Palladium supply was expected to face deficits in 2025 and 2026, continuing an ongoing supply-demand imbalance.
Major palladium producing countries like South Africa and Russia were facing disruptions due to strikes, energy shortages, and geopolitical tensions.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: The deal likely aimed to address the supply-demand imbalance in the palladium market.
Glencore's partnership with Lifezone Metals to recycle palladium could enhance supply stability amid declining mine production.
The strategic focus on critical green transportation metals aligns with the growing demand for sustainable solutions in the automotive industry.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal was likely aimed at addressing the supply-demand imbalance in the palladium market, which indicates a need for improved supply chain resilience. The mention of geopolitical tensions and disruptions in major producing countries further emphasizes the necessity for a more stable supply chain in the face of these challenges.
Secondary Driver Justification: The strategic focus on critical green transportation metals suggests an intent to innovate and adapt to the evolving automotive industry, particularly with the rise of electric vehicles. This aligns with the need for technology acquisition to support sustainable solutions, enhancing the company's capabilities in a changing market.
rationale:
1. Sustainable Operations: The text discusses GT Resources Inc.'s exploration and development of various mineral resources, including those critical for the sustainable transition to electric vehicles (EVs). This aligns with the intent of decarbonizing and optimizing operations to deliver lower emissions and higher efficiency.
2. Logic: The emphasis on addressing supply-demand imbalance in the palladium market and the partnership for recycling palladium showcases a commitment to sustainability within operational practices rather than mere exploration or resource extraction.
3. Evidence: The mention of strategic focus on critical green transportation metals and enhancing supply stability reinforces the notion of embedding sustainability metrics into day-to-day operations.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: As of December 1, 2023, Mineracão Rio do Norte S.A. and Alunorte - Alumina do Norte do Brasil S.A. were acquired by Glencore plc. Mineracão Rio do Norte S.A./ Alunorte - Alumina do Norte do Brasil S.A. represents the combined operations of Mineracão Rio do Norte S.A. and Alunorte - Alumina do Norte do Brasil S.A. in their sale to Norsk Hydro ASA. Mineracão Rio do Norte S.A. refines and produces alumina. Alunorte - Alumina do Norte do Brasil S.A. engages in the mining, production, beneficiation, supply, and sale of bauxite. Mineracão Rio do Norte S.A. and Alunorte - Alumina do Norte do Brasil S.A. are based in Brazil.
Deal Resolution: Glencore plc (LSE:GLEN) completed the acquisition of 45% stake in Mineracão Rio do Norte S.A. and 30% stake in Alunorte S.A. from Norsk Hydro ASA (OB:NHY) on December 1, 2023.
Target/Issuer: Mineracão Rio do Norte S.A./ Alunorte - Alumina do Norte do Brasil S.A.
Transaction Comments:
Glencore plc (LSE:GLEN) signed an agreement to acquire 45% stake in Mineracão Rio do Norte S.A. and 30% stake in Alunorte S.A. from Norsk Hydro ASA (OB:NHY) for $1.1 billion on April 27, 2023. Glencore is acquiring these non-controlling stakes for a combined equity value of $775 million, basis an agreed proportionate look through Enterprise Value and a net debt of $335 million in the business as at March 31, 2023. The transaction has an effective date of June 30, 2023 and includes certain post-closing adjustments, based on the performance of Alunorte over a 21-month period from June 30, 2023. The total payment on completion, including earn-in and other adjustments, is currently expected to be $700 million. Completion is expected to occur in H2 2023. Completion of the transaction is conditional on, among others, customary regulatory approvals and the two transactions with Hydro are inter-conditional. Proceeds will be used for strategic growth investments in line with Hydro’s 2025 strategy and for shareholder distribution. 

Robbie McLaren, Nick Cline, Craig Nethercott, Helen Lethaby, James Leslie and Veirano Advogados of Latham & Watkins LLP acted as legal advisor to Norsk Hydro ASA. Hydro receives competition clearing for Alunorte partnership with Glencore. The parties have received unconditional approval from relevant competition authorities, including Brazil, China, Germany and Austria, and there are no outstanding regulatory conditions to complete the transaction. The transactions are expected to be completed within November 30 or December 1, 2023.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Mining companies have long neglected copper exploration and are expanding their portfolios primarily through acquisitions.
Copper project prices in the exploration phase are rising, as evidenced by recent deals.
Rio Tinto's share price had been on a strong bull run due to rising commodity prices, particularly copper.
Glencore's stock price fluctuated following the failed acquisition attempt by Rio Tinto.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: The merger discussions were driven by the industry's shift towards repositioning portfolios for future-facing minerals.
Both companies aimed to capitalize on the strong uptrend in commodity prices, especially copper.
The deal was seen as a strategic move to enhance growth and operational efficiency in a competitive market.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal was driven by the industry's shift towards repositioning portfolios for future-facing minerals, indicating a clear intent to diversify the companies' offerings beyond their current operations. This is further supported by the pre-deal situation where mining companies are expanding their portfolios primarily through acquisitions, highlighting the need for diversification in response to market trends.
Secondary Driver Justification: The deal was also seen as a strategic move to enhance growth and operational efficiency in a competitive market. This suggests that improving operational efficiency was a significant consideration, as both companies aimed to capitalize on the strong uptrend in commodity prices, particularly copper, which requires efficient operations to maximize profitability.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Operations: The text discusses the acquisition of mining companies and their strategic shift towards future-facing minerals, particularly copper, which is relevant in the context of optimizing resource use and operational efficiency. 2. Logic: The focus on enhancing growth and operational efficiency indicates a commitment to sustainable practices in the mining industry. 3. Evidence: The mention of Glencore's strategy to capitalize on commodity prices and improve operations aligns with sustainable operational goals.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: GT Resources Inc. engages in the exploration and development of mineral resource properties in Canada and Finland. It primarily explores for nickel, copper, cobalt, platinum group elements, gold, palladium, and zinc deposits. The company was formerly known as Palladium One Mining Inc. and changed its name to GT Resources Inc. in March 2024. GT Resources Inc. is headquartered in Toronto, Canada.
Deal Resolution: On April 12, 2023, Palladium One Mining Inc. closed the transaction. The transaction included participation from 1 placee.
Transaction Comments: PalladiumOne Mining announced that it has entered into subscription agreement with a wholly owned subsidiary of new investor Glencore plc for non-brokered private placement of 28,347,000 common shares at a price of CAD 0.15 per common share for the gross proceeds of CAD 4,252,050. Glencore will own approximately 9.99% of the issued and outstanding common shares on a non-diluted basis. The private placement is expected to close on or about April 11, 2023. The transaction is subject to customary conditions, including acceptance by the TSX Venture Exchange. The common shares issued will be subject to a four-month hold period from the date of issuance in accordance with applicable securities laws.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Growing demand for palladium driven by its use in catalytic converters and a supply deficit.
High prices for palladium led to substitution with platinum in some applications.
Declining demand from the automotive sector due to a shift towards electric vehicles.
Mine disruptions in South Africa affecting palladium supply.
Sanctions on Russian palladium due to geopolitical tensions impacting global supply.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: To secure a reliable source of palladium amidst ongoing supply deficits.
To leverage Glencore's marketing and offtake capabilities for palladium recycling.
To enhance GT Resources' portfolio in critical green transportation metals.
To capitalize on the expected transition of the palladium market into a surplus contingent on recycling supply growth.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal was driven by the need to 'secure a reliable source of palladium amidst ongoing supply deficits,' indicating a strategic focus on acquiring essential resources. Additionally, the mention of leveraging Glencore's capabilities for palladium recycling further emphasizes the intent to enhance resource acquisition and exploration efforts in a challenging supply environment.
Secondary Driver Justification: The deal also aimed 'to enhance GT Resources' portfolio in critical green transportation metals,' which suggests a diversification strategy. This aligns with the company's goal to adapt to changing market demands, particularly in light of the declining automotive sector and the shift towards electric vehicles.
rationale: 1. Energy Transition: The focus on palladium in the context of declining automotive demand due to a shift towards electric vehicles indicates a direct relevance to the energy transition in the automotive sector. The text discusses the growing demand for palladium and its supply issues amidst the transition to greener technologies.** 2. Evidence: Phrases like 'shifting towards electric vehicles' and 'to enhance GT Resources' portfolio in critical green transportation metals' support this classification, as they indicate the company's alignment with evolving energy practices in response to market demands.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Mines copper, nickel, cobalt, platinum, paladium, gold, silver.
Deal Resolution:
Glencore plc, the Switzerland based integrated commodities producer and marketer. Involved in the exploration, extraction, production, processing, sourcing, refining, storage, financing and supply of metals and minerals, energy products and agricultural products is to acquire through its subsidiary Glencore AG, the local international trader of commodities and raw materials 17.94% of PolyMet Mining Corp, the Canada based mines copper, nickel, cobalt, platinum, paladium, gold, silver. 

The consideration was $74.1136m based on transfer of 35,124,919 shares at an offer price of $2.11 per share.

17 Jul 23 - The parties have signed a definitive agreement for the transaction. The consideration was $74.1136m based on transfer of 35,124,919 shares at an offer price of $2.11 per share.

The transaction is subject to approval by PolyMet shareholders, court approval and other customary closing conditions and is expected to be completed in in the late third quarter or early fourth quarter of 2023.

02 Nov 23 - PolyMet Mining Corp's shareholders approve the transaction.

03 Nov 23 - PolyMet Mining Corp has received the approval from the Supreme Court of British Columbia for the transaction. 

The transaction is expected to be completed on 07 Nov 2023.

07 Nov 23 - The transaction is completed.
Transaction Comments: Glencore plc acquired a 0.1791% stake in PolyMet Mining Corp (17.91%) on July 03, 2023.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Polymet Mining was involved in a complex permitting process for the NorthMet project in Minnesota.
The project faced legal hurdles that halted progress despite rising global demand for copper and nickel.
Polymet Mining's stock was struggling, with a significant decline in value over the past year.
The company had lost its trading status on stock exchanges, indicating severe financial distress.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Glencore aimed to consolidate its ownership of Polymet Mining, having already held a majority stake.
The acquisition allowed Glencore to eliminate the remaining public shares and take full control of the company.
By acquiring Polymet, Glencore could potentially streamline operations and address the ongoing permitting challenges more effectively.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal represents Glencore's strategy to consolidate its ownership of PolyMet Mining, as it already held a majority stake. By acquiring the remaining public shares, Glencore is effectively restructuring the ownership of PolyMet to streamline decision-making and operations, particularly in light of the company's financial distress and permitting challenges.
Secondary Driver Justification: The acquisition allows Glencore to potentially streamline operations and address the ongoing permitting challenges more effectively. This indicates a focus on improving operational efficiency, especially given PolyMet's complex permitting process and legal hurdles that have previously hindered progress.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Operations: The text describes Glencore's acquisition of PolyMet Mining, aiming to streamline operations and address ongoing permitting challenges. This aligns with the intent of optimizing manufacturing and operational systems to enhance sustainability metrics. 2. Evidence: The sentence "the acquisition allowed Glencore to eliminate the remaining public shares and take full control of the company" indicates a focus on operational efficiency, which is a core aspect of sustainable operations.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Operator of steelmaking coal properties.
Deal Resolution:
12 Jun 23 - Glencore plc, the Switzerland based integrated commodities producer and marketer, has made a proposal to acquire the steelmaking coal business from Teck Resources Ltd, the Canada based diversified natural resources company, focused on coal, natural gas, copper, zinc, gold, silver and niobium mining and development.

The consideration is expected to be $8.2bn as quoted in The Globe and Mail.

17 Aug 23 - JSW Steel Ltd, the listed India based integrated steel producer, is reported to be bidding for the business from Teck.

14 Nov 23 - Glencore agreed to acquire the steelmaking coal business, which is Elk Valley Resources Ltd (EVR), for $6.93bn.

Concurrently, Nippon Steel Corp, the listed Japanese integrated steel manufacturer, will exchange its 2.5% interest in Elkview Operations with $1.3bn cash and $0.4bn cashflow for 20% stake in EVR; whereas POSCO, the listed South Korean counterpart, will exchange its 2.5% interest in Elkview Operations and its 20% interest and 20% interest in the Greenhills joint venture, for 3% stake in EVR.

EVR is valued at an enterprise value of $9bn.

The transaction is subject to mandatory regulatory approvals, being Investment Canada Act (“ICA”) and competition approvals.  The transaction is expected to close in Q3 2024.

4 July 24 - The transaction has received regulatory approval after a successful review under the Investment Canada Act.

11 Jul 24 - Deal completed.
Transaction Comments: Glencore plc acquired a 0.77% stake in Elk Valley Resources Ltd (77%) on November 14, 2023.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Lincoln County was attempting to weaken water quality standards related to selenium at Lake Koocanusa.
The Montana Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ) had denied Lincoln County's petition to roll back existing water quality criteria due to public support for the current standards.
Environmental groups, including the Montana Environmental Information Center (MEIC), were actively opposing Lincoln County's efforts and sought transparency regarding communications with Glencore.
There was a history of contamination from Canadian coal mining operations affecting the watershed, prompting protective measures in Montana.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Glencore, through Elk Valley Resources, aimed to influence local regulations to facilitate mining operations without stringent environmental restrictions.
Lincoln County commissioners expressed concerns that existing selenium standards could hinder economic growth and development in the region.
The deal was likely motivated by a desire to align local governance with the interests of Glencore, which has a vested interest in the mining operations upstream from Lake Koocanusa.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal was primarily motivated by Glencore's aim to influence local regulations to facilitate mining operations without stringent environmental restrictions. The Pre_Deal_Situation indicates that Lincoln County was attempting to weaken water quality standards, which aligns with Glencore's interests in the region's mining operations, suggesting a strong focus on regulatory alignment to support their business objectives.
Secondary Driver Justification: The acquisition of Elk Valley Resources represents a geographic expansion for Glencore, as it seeks to enhance its operations in the steelmaking coal sector. The deal involves significant assets in Canada, which would allow Glencore to broaden its footprint in the North American market, thereby increasing its operational reach and resource base.
rationale: 1. Water stewardship: The text highlights the issues surrounding water quality standards in the context of coal mining operations, specifically mentioning Lincoln County's attempts to weaken these standards. The Montana Department of Environmental Quality's (DEQ) denial of the petition reflects a concern for water quality and watershed management, which aligns with the definition of water stewardship. 2. Evidence: Lincoln County was attempting to weaken water quality standards related to selenium at Lake Koocanusa, and environmental groups opposed this, emphasizing the importance of water quality management.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: An energy and chemicals park engaged in producing low-carbon energy products like biofuels, waste plastics for feedstock and renewable energy.
Buyers/Investors:
PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk
Glencore plc
Deal Resolution:
03 May 24 -  PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk and Glencore plc are reportedly interested in acquiring Shell Plc's oil refinery assets in Singapore.

The expected consideration is about $1bn as reported by Bloomberg. 

08 May 24 - PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk, the Indonesia based manufacturer of petrochemicals through its subsidiary CAPGC Pte Ltd, the Singapore based joint venture that is majority owned and operated by Chandra Asri Group and minority-owned by Glencore with interests is to acquire Shell Energy & Chemicals Park Singapore, the Singapore based owner and operator of refinery with processing capacity of 237,000 barrels per day of crude oil, a 1.1 million metric ton per annum ethylene cracker on Bukom island, and downstream chemical assets on Jurong Island from Shell plc, the United Kingdom based integrated energy company.

The consideration is $1bn as reported by Bloomberg and and CommoPlast. 

The transaction is subject to regulatory approval and is expected to complete by the end of 2024. 

01 Apr 25 - The transaction is complete.
Transaction Comments:
PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk
Glencore plc acquired a 1% stake in Shell Energy & Chemicals Park Singapore (100%) on May 08, 2024.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Glencore and Chandra Asri Pacific were engaged in a joint venture.
The joint venture was seeking to market a $1 billion sustainability-linked syndicated loan.
There was an increasing interest in ESG-related financing in the region.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: The deal aimed to capitalize on the growing appeal of sustainability-linked financing.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: neustark AG provides carbon removal services by capturing, transporting, and permanently storing carbon dioxide in demolished concrete through mineralization technology. The company offers carbon dioxide removal services by enabling recyclers to inject CO2 into concrete granules, which are then used in recycled construction materials. It operates storage sites in several European countries and enters into multi-year agreements with corporate clients to remove specified amounts of CO2, providing verified and certified CDR credits. The company collaborates with partners in the construction and recycling industries to deploy its technology at commercial scale and provides transparent reporting on CO2 removal. The company serves corporate clients such as financial institutions, private equity firms, insurance companies, and businesses seeking net-zero emissions, as well as organizations in the construction and recycling sectors. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: neustark AG provides carbon removal services by capturing, transporting, and permanently storing carbon dioxide in demolished concrete through mineralization technology.
