The company serves corporate clients such as financial institutions, private equity firms, insurance companies, and businesses seeking net-zero emissions, as well as organizations in the construction and recycling sectors. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Amrize AG engages in the provision of various building solutions for infrastructure, commercial, and residential construction markets in North America. It operates through two segments, Building Materials and Building Envelope. The Building Materials segment offers cement and aggregates, as well as ready-mix concrete, asphalt, and other construction materials. The Building Envelope segment provides advanced roofing and wall systems, including single-ply membranes, insulation, shingles, sheathing, waterproofing, and protective coatings; and adhesives, tapes, and sealants. The company was incorporated in 2023 and is based in Zug, Switzerland.
Deal Resolution: The Board of Holcim Ltd (SWX:HOLN) completed the spin off of it's North American business in the US on June 23, 2025. Shares of Amrize will commence trading under the ticker symbol “AMRZ” on the SIX Swiss Exchange at 9:00 a.m. CEST and on the New York Stock Exchange at 9:30 a.m. ET as of June 23, 2025.
Transaction Comments:
The Board of Holcim Ltd (SWX:HOLN) announced the spin off of it's North American business in the US on January 28, 2024. The transaction is intended to be executed as a spin-off, with the final structure to be communicated in the second half of 2024. The Board has tasked Jan Jenisch to lead the planned US listing of Holcim’s North America business. The US listing is expected to be completed in the first half of 2025. It will be subject to shareholder approval at an Extraordinary General Meeting expected in the first quarter of 2025, as well as other customary approvals. As of December 6, 2024, The annual general meeting of shareholders is expected to be held on May 14, 2025. Transaction is expected to occur by the end of the first half 2025. As of January 10, 2025, Holcim has appointed Jan Jenisch as chairman and Chief Executive Officer of its North American company after its spin-off. As of February 21, 2025 Holcim’s North America business to be named Amrize following planned spin-off. The company intends to list Amrize shares under the symbol ‘‘AMRZ’’ on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) with an additional listing on the SIX Swiss Exchange. The Amrize name and branding will go into effect with the completion of the spin-off, expected by the end of the first half 2025, subject to shareholder and customary approvals. Until then, the North American business continues to operate as part of Holcim. As of February 8, 2025, Amrize Form 10 publicly filed with U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for planned spin-off. Following the planned spin-off, Amrize will be the largest building solutions company focused exclusively on the North American market, offering its customers a broad range of advanced building solutions from foundation to rooftop across all construction markets. As of March 25, 2025, Holcim takes the next step towards the planned spin-off of its North American Business, Amrize, with the appointment of the future company’s designated executive leadership team. The designated Amrize executive officers include Jan Jenisch, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, Ian Johnston, Chief Financial Officer, Jaime Hill, President of Building Materials, Jake Gosa, President of Building Envelope, Nollaig Forrest, Chief Marketing and Corporate Affairs Officer, Steve Clark, Chief People Officer, Denise Singleton, Chief Legal Officer & Corporate Secretary, Sam Poletti, Chief Strategy and M&A Officer, Mario Gross, Chief Supply Chain Officer and Roald Brouwer, Chief Technology Officer. As of April 14, 2025, if approved by shareholders at the AGM, the spin-off is expected to occur in June 2025, subject to customary approvals, including approval by Holcim shareholders at Holcim’s 2025 annual general meeting on May 14, 2025. As of May 14, 2025, Holcim shareholders backed the separation and spinoff of the cement maker's North American business. Shareholders approved the proposed special distribution by way of a dividend-in-kind of one Amrize Ltd share for every Holcim Ltd share to effect the spin-off. The shares of Amrize Ltd are expected to be listed on the New York Stock Exchange and on the SIX Swiss Exchange under the ticker symbol "AMRZ" in June. As of June 2, 2025, the transaction is expected to complete on June 20, 2025. Shares of Amrize are expected to commence trading under the ticker symbol “AMRZ” on the SIX Swiss Exchange and on the New York Stock Exchange on June 23, 2025. As of June 20, 2025, Amrize Ltd and Holcim Ltd entered into definitive agreement.

Evercore Group L.L.C., Goldman Sachs International and Perella Weinberg Partners are acting as financial advisors and Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom LLP and Urs Kägi, Vera Naegeli, Susanne Schreiber, Raoul Stocker, Lukas Roesler, Markus Wang, Laura Widmer, Dieter Dubs, Thomas Stoltz, Christian Kunz of Bär & Karrer AG are serving as legal advisors. Computershare Trust Company, National Association acted as transfer agent to Holcim. Merrill Lynch Capital Markets GmbH acted as financial advisor to Holcim.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Holcim's stock was under pressure due to profit-taking and EU emissions regulations.
Amrize was receiving mixed ratings from analysts, with a consensus recommendation of 'Moderate Buy'.
Amrize's stock performance showed volatility, with a 1-year low of $44.12 and a high of $60.57.

The transaction is subject to definitive agreement.

30 Sep 24 - The transaction is complete.
Transaction Comments: Nestle SA acquired a 0.0862% stake in Seres Therapeutics Inc (8.62%) on August 06, 2024.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Seres Therapeutics was struggling financially and had to cut its staff by 30%.
The company was focusing resources on earlier, immunology-focused research.
Seres had advanced key startup activities for the SER-155 Phase 2 study but paused further investment.
The company was seeking funding for the SER-155 study while extending its cash runway through Q3 2026.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Nestlé acquired VOWST™, the first FDA-approved orally administered microbiome biotherapeutic, from Seres, indicating confidence in Seres' technology.
The acquisition aligns with Nestlé's interest in expanding its health science portfolio.
Seres aimed to leverage the acquisition to secure additional funding and support for its ongoing and future biotherapeutic programs.
Primary Driver Justification: Nestlé's acquisition of Seres Therapeutics is primarily driven by its interest in Seres' technology, as evidenced by the statement that 'Nestlé acquired VOWST™, the first FDA-approved orally administered microbiome biotherapeutic, from Seres, indicating confidence in Seres' technology.' This highlights the strategic intent to enhance Nestlé's capabilities in the biotherapeutic space.
Secondary Driver Justification: The deal also serves to diversify Nestlé's health science portfolio, as indicated by the reason that 'the acquisition aligns with Nestlé's interest in expanding its health science portfolio.' This suggests that Nestlé is looking to broaden its offerings in the health sector through this investment.
rationale: 1. Customer health and wellness: The text discusses a biopharmaceutical company, Seres Therapeutics, focusing on developing biological drugs that modulate the microbiome to treat diseases. This aligns closely with enhancing customer health and wellness as it pertains to medical products. Evidence: The text states that these drugs are designed to treat diseases, which directly relates to customer health and wellness.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Company engaged in the business of trading and dealing in nutritional products.
Deal Resolution:
Nestle (Malaysia) Bhd, the Malaysia based it is an investment holding company. Its subsidiaries are engaged in the manufacture, marketing and the sale, both locally and for export of sweetened condensed milk, powdered milk and drinks, liquid milk and juices, instant coffee, instant noodles, culinary products, confectionary and cereals and yoghurt and related products, is to acquire 27.39% of Wyeth Nutrition (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd, the local company engaged in the business of trading and dealing in nutritional products from Nestle (Malaysia)'s parent company Nestle SA, the Switzerland based manufacturer of food products including confectionery, prepared dishes and cooking aids, milk-based products, cereals, instant coffee, pharmaceuticals, pet care products and baby foods. 

The consideration was 45.19m MYR ($10.18m).

The transaction is subject to fulfilment of the conditions precedent. The Proposed Acquisition is expected to be completed by the 2nd quarter of 2023.

31-Jul-23 -  Nestle highlighted that it did not include the revenue and earnings contribution from Wyeth Nutrition Malaysia in the 2QFY23 results as the acquisition was completed on 30 June 2023.
Transaction Comments: Nestle (Malaysia) Bhd acquired a 0.2739% stake in Wyeth Nutrition (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd (27.39%) on February 22, 2023.
Pre_Deal_Situation: There was a growing contamination scare regarding Nestle infant milk formula in Hong Kong.
Reports indicated that at least 23 batches of powdered infant and young children's formula were affected.
Authorities received about 50 reports of children feeling unwell after consuming the affected formula.
The contamination was linked to the presence of cereulide, a toxin produced by Bacillus cereus.
Nestle had already initiated a precautionary recall of 22 batches of its products.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: The deal likely aimed to address the contamination issues and restore consumer trust.
Combining resources and expertise could enhance product safety and quality control measures.
The partnership may provide a strategic advantage in navigating regulatory challenges and improving market position.
Collaborating could help both companies manage the fallout from the contamination scare more effectively.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal is primarily aimed at addressing the contamination issues that have affected consumer trust in Nestle's products. By acquiring a stake in Wyeth Nutrition, Nestle can combine resources and expertise to enhance product safety and quality control measures, thereby strengthening its competitive position in the market amidst the fallout from the contamination scare.
Secondary Driver Justification: The acquisition also serves a regulatory alignment purpose, as it may provide Nestle with a strategic advantage in navigating the regulatory challenges posed by the contamination issues. Collaborating with Wyeth Nutrition could help both companies manage compliance and improve their standing with regulatory authorities, which is crucial in restoring consumer confidence.
rationale: 1. Customer health and wellness: The text discusses a contamination scare involving Nestle's infant milk formula that affected children's health, indicating that the safety and health of customers were compromised. It outlines the company's response, including a precautionary recall, which aims to protect consumer well-being. 2. Logic: The focus is on ensuring that products are safe for consumers, directly aligning with the goal of enhancing customer health and wellness. 3. Evidence: The text states that there were reports of children feeling unwell after consuming the contaminated formula, highlighting the potential health risks to customers.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Manufacturer of honey.

05 Dec 24 - Nestle SA, the listed Switzerland based manufacturer of food products including confectionery, prepared dishes and cooking aids, milk-based products, cereals, instant coffee, pharmaceuticals, pet care products and baby foods, is reported to be seeking buyers for Egmont Honey Ltd, the New Zealand based manufacturer of honey. The terms of the deal were undisclosed.

05 Dec 24 - Huatai Zijin Investment Co Ltd, a Chinese private equity firm, has agreed to acquire Egmont Honey. Terms were not disclosed.

26 Mar 25 - Overseas Investment Office has made a decision to allow the sale of Egmont Honey to Huatai Zijin Investment. The consideration was over 100m NZD ($58.7m).

10 Apr 25 - Huatai Zijin Investment has acquired 90% of Egmont Honey on 1 Apr 2025.
Transaction Comments: Huatai Zijin Investment Co Ltd acquired a 0.9% stake in Egmont Honey Ltd (90%) on December 05, 2024.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Nestle owned Egmont Honey for less than two years after acquiring it as part of The Better Health Company.
Egmont Honey was recognized as the fastest-growing manuka honey company globally, with impressive growth over the past two years.
The company was expected to generate significant revenue and earnings in the upcoming fiscal year.
Nestle was reviewing the future of its honey business and considering divestment.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Nestle claimed that Egmont Honey was no longer a strategic match for its business.
The sale aligns with Egmont Honey's expansion strategy in China, which Huatai International can assist with.
Nestle sought to divest the business with the support of investment banks to find suitable buyers.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal aligns with Egmont Honey's expansion strategy in China, which Huatai International can assist with, indicating a clear intent for geographic expansion into a new market. Nestle's decision to divest suggests that they no longer see strategic value in the honey business, allowing Huatai Zijin Investment to leverage Egmont Honey's growth potential in China.
Secondary Driver Justification: Egmont Honey was recognized as the fastest-growing manuka honey company globally, indicating a strong market position that Huatai Zijin Investment can capitalize on. The acquisition allows Huatai to expand its market share in the honey sector, particularly in a high-growth region like China, where demand for manuka honey is increasing.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Business Building: The rationale for classifying this text under 'Sustainable Business Building' is based on Nestle's divestment of Egmont Honey to focus on sustainable growth opportunities, specifically targeting the expansion of the honey business in China. The evidence indicates that the acquisition aligns with a strategy to facilitate expansion in a significant market, which fits the idea of creating sustainable business models that capture new profit pools. **The decision for Huatai Zijin Investment to acquire Egmont Honey is rooted in a strategic alignment with its expansion plans in China, showing how the deal supports sustainable business growth. This matches the definition of Sustainable Business Building as it supports creating new profit opportunities, particularly in sectors like health food and wellness through honey products.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Celsius Holdings, Inc. develops, processes, manufactures, markets, sells, and distributes functional energy drinks in the United States, North America, Europe, the Asia Pacific, and internationally. The company offers CELSIUS, a lifestyle and energy drink; CELSIUS Originals and Vibe, which are functional energy drinks in various flavors that is offered in carbonated and non-carbonated forms; CELSIUS ESSENTIALS, a functional energy drink formulated with aminos; CELSIUS On-the-Go Powder, a functional energy drink in powder form; and CELSIUS Hydration, a line of zero-sugar hydration powders infused electrolytes in various fruit-forward flavors, as well as CELSIUS ready-to-drink products. It distributes its products through direct-to-store delivery, independent distributors, supermarkets, convenience stores, drug stores, nutritional stores, food service providers, and mass merchants, as well as natural food stores, fitness centers, mass market retailers, vitamin specialty stores, club stores, gyms, and e-commerce platforms. The company was formerly known as Vector Ventures, Inc. and changed its name to Celsius Holdings, Inc. in January 2007. Celsius Holdings, Inc. was founded in 2004 and is headquartered in Boca Raton, Florida.
Deal Resolution: On August 28, 2025, Celsius Holdings, Inc. (NasdaqCM:CELH) closed the transaction.
Transaction Comments:
Celsius Holdings, Inc entered into a subscription agreement to issue 390,000 shares of Series B Convertible Preferred Stock at an issue price of $1,500 for the aggregate proceeds of $585.0 on August 28, 2025. The transaction involves participation of  PepsiCo as an investor and will increase its Stake from 8.5% to 11% and will get the right to nominate an additional board member. The conversion price of Series B stock is $28.99 and Dividends will accrue on each share of Series B Preferred Stock at the rate of 5.00% per annum. Subject to the satisfaction of certain conditions, as set forth in the Certificate of Designation of Series B Convertible Preferred Stock, setting forth the rights, preferences, privileges and restrictions applicable to the Series B Preferred Stock (the “ Series B Certificate ”), each share of Series B Preferred Stock is initially convertible into 11,304,348 shares of Common Stock. The Company offered and sold the shares of Series B Preferred Stock to PepsiCo in reliance on the exemption from registration under the Securities Act provided by Section 4(a)(2) thereof. Under the Series B Purchase Agreement, PepsiCo represented that it is an “accredited investor” as defined in Rule 501(a) under the Securities Act and that it was acquiring the shares of Series B Preferred Stock for investment purposes and not with a view to, or for sale in connection with, any distribution thereof in violation of the Securities Act.

UBS Securities LLC and Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC acted as a placement agent. Ethan Klingsberg; Sanjay Murti of Freshfields US LLP acted as legal advisor to company and Claudia J. Ricciardi of Cravath, Swaine & Moore LLP acted as legal advisor to investor in the transaction.
Pre_Deal_Situation: PepsiCo was experiencing positive sentiment due to its dividend yield and steady payout, attracting investor interest.
Analysts highlighted PepsiCo as a top long-term growth name based on its portfolio and cash flow.
PepsiCo was balancing innovation spending with margin recovery, indicating a focus on growth.
The company was involved in debt capital markets activity, which raised leverage but secured funding.
PepsiCo was cutting prices on snacks to drive volume, which could impact near-term margins.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: The deal likely aimed to enhance PepsiCo's portfolio by incorporating Celsius Holdings, which aligns with consumer trends towards healthier beverage options.
Celsius Holdings' growth potential in the energy drink market could complement PepsiCo's existing product offerings.
The partnership may provide PepsiCo with increased market share and competitive advantage in the beverage sector.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal likely aimed to enhance PepsiCo's market share by incorporating Celsius Holdings, which aligns with consumer trends towards healthier beverage options. Analysts noted that PepsiCo was balancing innovation spending with margin recovery, indicating a focus on growth and competitive advantage in the beverage sector.

The deal complements PepsiCo's existing product offerings and aligns with the trend towards healthier beverages, thus enhancing its overall portfolio.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: VNGR Beverage, LLC, doing business as Poppi, operates as a prebiotic soda brand that produces and sells fruit juice infused with apple cider vinegar. It offers ginger lime, strawberry lemon, raspberry rose, orange, watermelon, cola, root beer, grapefruit, pineapple, and other flavors. The company sells its products through stores and online. The company was incorporated in 2015 and is based in Austin, Texas. As of May 19, 2025, VNGR Beverage LLC operates as a subsidiary of PepsiCo, Inc.
Deal Resolution: PepsiCo, Inc. (NasdaqGS:PEP) completed the acquisition of VNGR Beverage LLC ("Poppi") from CAVU Consumer Partners,LLC and others on May 19, 2025.
Pre-Deal Situation: PepsiCo, Inc. (NasdaqGS:PEP) is in advanced talks to buy healthier soda brand Poppi (VNGR Beverage LLC), according to people with knowledge of the matter. The Purchase, New York-based beverage giant could announce the transaction as soon as next week, said one of the people, who asked not to be identified discussing confidential information. The purchase price under discussion is more than $1.5 billion, the person said. PepsiCo had planned to launch its own so-called functional soda under the brand Soulboost, but decided to scrap that effort because of early indicators it wouldn’t succeed. While deliberations are at a late stage, they could still be delayed, the people said. A representative for PepsiCo declined to comment. A spokesperson for Poppi didn’t respond to a request for comment.
Transaction Comments:
PepsiCo, Inc. (NasdaqGS:PEP) entered into a definitive agreement to acquire VNGR Beverage LLC ("Poppi") from CAVU Consumer Partners,LLC and others for $1.9 billion on March 16, 2025. PepsiCo agreed to acquire Poppi for $1.95 billion, including $300 million of anticipated cash tax benefits for a net purchase price of $1.65 billion. The transaction also includes an additional potential earnout consideration subject to the achievement of certain performance milestones within a specified period after closing of the transaction. The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, including regulatory approval.

Centerview Partners LLC is acting as lead financial advisor to PepsiCo, and J.P. Morgan Securities LLC is also acting as a financial advisor to PepsiCo. Cravath, Swaine & Moore LLP is acting as legal advisor to PepsiCo, and Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP is acting as tax counsel to PepsiCo. Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC is acting as financial advisor to poppi, and Nick Davis, Erin Kirchner,John Robertson,Colleen Badgley,Megan Browdie, Jonathan Rivinus and Keith Berets of Cooley LLP is acting as legal advisor to poppi.
Pre_Deal_Situation: VNGR Beverage, the former owner of Poppi, was involved in a class-action lawsuit regarding misleading marketing claims.
The lawsuit claimed that Poppi's beverages were marketed as 'gut healthy' without scientific evidence.
VNGR Beverage agreed to settle the lawsuit for $8.9 million but admitted no wrongdoing.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: PepsiCo aimed to expand its portfolio in the functional drinks market by acquiring Poppi.
The acquisition was valued at $1.95 billion, indicating a significant investment in a growing beverage category.
Primary Driver Justification: PepsiCo aimed to expand its portfolio in the functional drinks market by acquiring Poppi, indicating a strategic move to diversify its offerings in a growing beverage category. This aligns with the intent to enhance its product range and cater to evolving consumer preferences for health-oriented beverages.
Secondary Driver Justification: The acquisition of Poppi, valued at $1.95 billion, suggests that PepsiCo is also focused on increasing its market share within the functional drinks segment. By integrating Poppi's prebiotic soda brand, PepsiCo can strengthen its position in a competitive market and attract a broader customer base.
rationale: 1. Customer health and wellness: The text discusses Poppi, a brand that markets prebiotic soda as beneficial for gut health. Despite facing a lawsuit regarding its marketing claims, the focus remains on the product's health benefits, specifically its infusion of apple cider vinegar, a known ingredient for promoting digestive health. The description of various flavors also suggests a consumer-oriented approach aimed at enhancing health and wellness. This aligns well with the class of 'Customer health and wellness' as the emphasis here is on products designed to improve consumer well-being, even amid controversy. **2. Evidence: The narrative highlights the nature of Poppi's products being marketed as 'gut healthy,' which directly relates to the customer health aspect, suggesting an intent to promote wellness and health benefits through their offerings.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Sabra Dipping Co., LLC produces and distributes hummus, guacamole, Greek yogurt dips, salsas, and vegetarian sides. It sells its products through grocery stores in the United States and Canada. The company was formerly known as Sabra Blue & White Foods, LLC and changed its name to Sabra Dipping Co., LLC in March 2008. The company was founded in 1986 and is based in White Plains, New York. Sabra Dipping Co., LLC operates as a joint venture between Strauss Fresh Foods Ltd. and Pepsico, Inc. As of December 3, 2024, Sabra Dipping Co., LLC operates as a subsidiary of PepsiCo, Inc.
Deal Resolution: PepsiCo, Inc. (NasdaqGS:PEP) completed the acquisition of remaining 50% stake in Sabra Dipping Co., LLC  from S.E USA, Inc. for approximately $240 million on December 3, 2024. Transaction Comments:
PepsiCo, Inc. (NasdaqGS:PEP) entered into agreement to acquire remaining 50% stake in Sabra Dipping Co., LLC  from S.E USA, Inc. for approximately $240 million on November 21, 2024. The sale consideration, to be paid in cash on the date of completion, amounts to $240.8 million is for the sale of the holdings in Sabra (including the assignment of Company's shareholder loan to Sabra). Upon completion, PepsiCo, Inc. will own 100% stake in Sabra Dipping Co., LLC. 
The transactions are subject to customary closing conditions and are expected to close by the end of 2024. In separate transaction, PepsiCo, Inc. (NasdaqGS:PEP) also agreed to acquire remaining 50% stake in Fresh Dips & Spreads International GmbH from Strauss Group Ltd. (TASE:STRS). Sabra and Obela have approximately 700 employees (as of the end of 2023).

In addition, Strauss will receive an option to purchase up to 2.5% of PepsiCo’s holdings in the salty snacks division in Israel.

The company was actively acquiring healthier brands to align with changing consumer preferences towards health and wellness.

Why_The_Deal_Happened: PepsiCo aimed to fully own the Sabra and Obela brands to strengthen its market share in the refrigerated fresh hummus segment.
The acquisition was part of PepsiCo's broader strategy to enhance its portfolio with healthier options in response to consumer demand.
Owning Sabra would allow PepsiCo to leverage its distribution and marketing capabilities to further grow the brand.
Primary Driver Justification: PepsiCo aimed to fully own the Sabra and Obela brands to strengthen its market share in the refrigerated fresh hummus segment. This indicates a clear intent to increase their presence and competitiveness in a growing market segment.
Secondary Driver Justification: The acquisition was part of PepsiCo's broader strategy to enhance its portfolio with healthier options in response to consumer demand. This aligns with PepsiCo's focus on diversifying its product offerings away from traditional soda and snacks, indicating a strategic move towards a more health-conscious product lineup.
rationale: 1. Evidence: The text states that 'PepsiCo was focusing on diversifying its product offerings away from traditional soda and snacks' and mentions consumer preferences for health-related products.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Maplebear Inc., doing business as Instacart, engages in the provision of online grocery shopping services to households in North America. Its service can be provided through company’s mobile application or website. The company also provides advertising services; and software-as-a-service solutions. Maplebear Inc., was incorporated in 2012 and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.
Deal Resolution: On September 21, 2023, Maplebear Inc. closed the transaction.
Transaction Comments:
Maplebear Inc. announced that it entered into a securities purchase agreement for $175,000,000 total gross proceeds in the transaction on August 23, 2023. The transaction will include participation from new investor PepsiCo, Inc. The company will issue series A preferred stock with a par value $0.0001 per share. The series A preferred stock will have a conversion price equal to the initial public offering price per share. The shares are convertible, and redeemable. The purchase of the series A preferred stock is contingent upon, and will occur immediately subsequent to, the closing of the company’s initial public offering, subject to the satisfaction of certain additional conditions to closing. The sale of these shares will not be registered in this offering and will be subject to certain restrictions on transfer. The placement agent will receive a placement agent fee equal to 1.5% of the series A preferred stock sold in the transaction.

Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC will act as placement agent to the company and Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP acted as a legal advisor to PepsiCo, Inc. in the transaction.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: To enhance customer shopping experience through gamification and personalized offers.
To expand capabilities in partnership with retailers and brands.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: As of October 1, 2024, PepsiCo's activities in Belgium and Luxembourg was acquired by Royal Unibrew A/S. PepsiCo's activities in Belgium and Luxembourg engages in manufacture, marketing, distribution, and sale of various beverages and convenient foods worldwide. The asset is located in the United States.
Deal Resolution: Royal Unibrew A/S (CPSE:RBREW) completed the acquisition of PepsiCo’s activities in Belgium and Luxembourg from PepsiCo, Inc. (NasdaqGS:PEP) on October 1, 2024.
Target/Issuer: PepsiCo's activities in Belgium and Luxembourg
Transaction Comments: Royal Unibrew A/S (CPSE:RBREW) acquired PepsiCo’s activities in Belgium and Luxembourg from PepsiCo, Inc. (NasdaqGS:PEP) on October 1, 2024. Arnaud Van Oekel, Sophie Berg, Renaud Dupont and Annabelle Lepièce of CMS Belgium acted as legal advisors to Royal Unibrew on their acquisition.
Pre_Deal_Situation: High taxation on sugary drinks in France leading to significant fraud.
Organized fraud by temporary wholesalers depriving the state of hundreds of millions of euros annually.
Serious market distortion affecting compliant businesses and eroding their margins.
Authorities' inaction and lack of effective control mechanisms exacerbating the situation.
Major beverage companies, including PepsiCo, remained silent despite being disadvantaged by the fraud.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: To address the urgent need for compliance and protect market share against fraudulent practices.
To enhance strategic positioning in a market increasingly affected by unfair competition.
To implement measures that ensure transparency and accountability in the supply chain.
To foster a more equitable market environment by advocating for shared responsibility among all stakeholders.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal was driven by the need to 'enhance strategic positioning in a market increasingly affected by unfair competition,' indicating a focus on strengthening competitive positioning against fraudulent practices that distort the market. This aligns with the urgency to protect market share and ensure compliance in a challenging environment.
Secondary Driver Justification: The acquisition aims 'to address the urgent need for compliance and protect market share against fraudulent practices,' which suggests that expanding market share was a significant consideration. By acquiring PepsiCo's activities, Royal Unibrew A/S can better position itself to capture market opportunities and mitigate the impact of fraud on its operations.
rationale: 1. Tax practices: The text discusses high taxation on sugary drinks in France and mentions significant fraud impacting the state financially. This directly relates to the responsibility of businesses, including PepsiCo, to navigate taxation and its implications on market behavior. **2. Logic: The text outlines a situation where the tax system led to fraudulent activities that undermine compliant businesses, indicating a link to tax practices. **3. Evidence: The statement 'High taxation on sugary drinks in France leading to significant fraud' suggests a challenge related to taxation policies affecting business operations within the market.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: International Dairy & Juice Limited engages in investment in dairy and juice processors in Southeast Asia, Africa, and the Middle East. The company was incorporated in 2009 and is based in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. International Dairy & Juice Limited operates as a subsidiary of Almarai Company.
Deal Resolution: Almarai Investment Holding Company W.L.L completed the acquisition of remaining 48% stake in International Dairy & Juice Limited from PepsiCo, Inc. (NasdaqGS:PEP) on February 17, 2023. Walkers acted as Bermuda legal counsel to PepsiCo, alongside Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer as lead counsel. J.P. Morgan Saudi Arabia Ltd acted as financial advisor to Almarai Investment Holding Company W.L.L.
Transaction Comments: Almarai Investment Holding Company W.L.L acquired remaining 48% stake in International Dairy & Juice Limited from PepsiCo, Inc. (NasdaqGS:PEP) for SAR 255 million on February 17, 2023. The full ownership of IDJ will allow Almarai to further expand strategically in the wider region. The purchase is funded from Almarai’s internal cash flows.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Almarai and PepsiCo had an existing joint venture in Egypt through International Company for Agro-Industrial Projects (Beyti).
Beyti was involved in selling dairy and juice products in Egypt.
Almarai owned 52% of Beyti, while PepsiCo owned the remaining 48%.
Egypt's economic and political conditions were uncertain prior to the deal.

rationale: 1. Sustainable Business Building: The focus of the text revolves around the acquisition and intended expansion of dairy and juice processing operations in Egypt. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Mineral water and soft drinks manufacturer.
Deal Resolution:
Mattoni 1873 as, the Czechia based mineral water manufacturer has acquired 46.43% of Knjaz Milos AD, the Serbia based mineral water and soft drinks manufacturer from PepsiCo Inc, the United States based producer of soft drinks and snack foods.

The consideration was 90m EUR ($96.261m).
Transaction Comments: Mattoni 1873 as acquired a 0.4643% stake in Knjaz Milos AD (46.43%) on April 24, 2024.
Pre_Deal_Situation: PepsiCo held a minority stake in Knjaz Miloš, a Serbian mineral water and energy drinks company.
Mattoni 1873 was a Czech soft-drinks business with interests in expanding its control over beverage companies in the region.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Mattoni 1873 aimed to gain sole control over Knjaz Miloš to strengthen its market position in the Serbian beverage sector.
The acquisition allowed Mattoni 1873 to eliminate a minority stakeholder, facilitating more streamlined decision-making and operational control.
Primary Driver Justification: Mattoni 1873 aimed to gain sole control over Knjaz Miloš to strengthen its market position in the Serbian beverage sector, indicating a clear intent to expand its market share in the region. The acquisition of a significant stake directly supports this goal by consolidating its presence in the market.
Secondary Driver Justification: By eliminating PepsiCo as a minority stakeholder, Mattoni 1873 facilitates more streamlined decision-making and operational control, which enhances its competitive positioning in the beverage sector. This move allows for more agile responses to market demands and strengthens its overall competitive stance.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Business Building: The text describes Mattoni 1873's strategic acquisition of Knjaz Milos AD to strengthen its market position within the beverage sector in Serbia. This aligns with creating new sustainable business models, capturing profit pools, and pursuing sustainable growth opportunities. **2. LOGIC: The acquisition is aimed at gaining sole control over the operations of Knjaz Milos, which indicates an intent to develop a more cohesive and sustainable business strategy in the region. Evidence: Phrases such as "to strengthen its market position" and "facilitating more streamlined decision-making and operational control" support this classification.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: I-Pulse Inc. develops and manufactures pulsed power-based technologies for the natural resources, global energy, manufacturing, and the research and development industries. The company's products include I-ROX, a pulsed power technology solution for breaking rocks in mines; i-mine, a continuous and scalable mining and minerals processing solution for rock faces; IPW Typhoon, a geophysical survey technology product to locate hidden water resources underground; G-Pulse, a drilling technology solution for the geothermal energy sector; Blue Spark Geothermal, a blockage clearing product for geothermal wells; and Bmax, a metal forming and welding solution for advanced manufacturing applications. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Bunker Hill Mining Corp. operates as a mineral exploration and development company in Coeur d'Alene Mining District, the Silver Valley. It focuses on exploring for zinc, lead, and silver ores. Its flagship asset is the 100% owned Bunker Hill mine located in the Silver Valley, Idaho. Bunker Hill Mining Corp. was formerly known as Liberty Silver Corp. and changed its name to Bunker Hill Mining Corp. in September 2017. The company was incorporated in 2007 and is headquartered in Vancouver, Canada.
Deal Resolution:
On September 29, 2025, Bunker Hill Mining Corp closed the transaction. The company issued 150,000,000 units at an issue price of CAD 0.12 for gross proceeds of CAD 18,000,000 under Canadian offering and issued 225,000,000 units at an issue price of CAD 0.12 for gross proceeds of CAD 27,000,000 under United States offering for aggregate proceeds of CAD 45,000,000. The company also issued additional 56,250,000 units under Canadian offering, which is part of full exercise of the underwriters’ over-allotment option. The transaction includes participation from Teck Resources Limited for 223,786,706 units. The securities issued under the offering are subject to a statutory hold period of four months and one day in accordance with applicable Canadian securities laws, due to expire on January 30, 2026, and to a minimum concurrent six-month hold period in accordance with applicable U.S. securities laws. Immediately prior to the date hereof, Teck beneficially owned, directly or indirectly, or exercised control or direction over, 219,079,378 common shares and warrants to purchase an additional 100,598,716 common shares, representing approximately 23.6% of the issued and outstanding common shares on a non-diluted basis and approximately 31.1% on a partially diluted basis. Upon closing of the offering, Teck now beneficially owns, directly or indirectly, or exercises control or direction over 442,866,084 common shares and warrants to purchase an additional 324,385,422 common shares, representing approximately 32.6% of the issued and outstanding common shares on a non-diluted basis and, assuming the exercise of all warrants now held by Teck, approximately 45.6% on a partially diluted basis.  

The company has received proceeds from 9 investors pursuant to Regulation D.
Transaction Comments:
Bunker Hill Mining Corp. announced that it has entered into an agreement with Haywood Securities Inc. in connection with bought deal private placement basis, to issue 375,000,000 units of the company at an issue price of CAD 0.12 for gross proceeds of CAD 45,000,000 on September 5, 2025. Each unit will consist of one share of common stock of the company and one common stock purchase warrant of the company. Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to acquire one share of common stock of the company at a price per warrant share of CAD 0.17 for a period of 60 months from the closing date. Company has agreed to grant the underwriters an option to purchase up to an additional 56,250,000 units at the issue price, exercisable in whole or in part at any time up to 48 hours prior to the closing date. A cornerstone investor is expected to subscribe for approximately CAD 27,461,560 in units under the offering. A separate cornerstone investor is expected to subscribe for approximately CAD 7,005,500. The offering is expected to close on September 29, 2025, and is subject to certain closing conditions including, but not limited to, the receipt of all necessary approvals including the conditional listing approval of the TSX Venture Exchange and the applicable securities regulatory authorities. The securities to be issued under the offering will be subject to statutory hold period of four months and one day in accordance with applicable Canadian securities laws and to a minimum concurrent six-month hold period in accordance with applicable U.S. securities laws. 

On September 15, 2025, the company amended the terms of the transaction. The company will now issue 150,000,000 units at an issue price of CAD 0.12 for gross proceeds of CAD 18,000,000 under Canadian offering and issued 225,000,000 units at an issue price of CAD 0.12 for gross proceeds of CAD 27,000,000 under United States offering for aggregate proceeds of CAD 45,000,000. In consideration of their services, the company has agreed to pay the underwriters a cash commission equal to 6% of the gross proceeds from the offering, provided that the cash fee will be 3% of the aggregate gross proceeds raised in the offering from certain purchasers listed on the president's list, and issue that number of non-transferable compensation options equal to up to 6% of aggregate number of units sold under the offering, provided that the number of compensation options will be equal to 3% the aggregate number of units issued by the company to certain purchasers on the president's list. In consideration of their services, the company may pay certain finders, including Zed Financial Partners, a cash commission equal to 4% of the aggregate gross proceeds raised from certain subscribers introduced by such finders to the company in the offering, and issue that number of compensation options as is equal to 4%of the aggregate number of units sold under the offering to subscribers introduced by the finders. Each compensation option is exercisable to acquire one share of common stock of the company at a price of CAD 0.12 per share for a period of 24 months from the closing date of the offering. 

ZED Financial Partners, BMO Capital Markets and Haywood Securities Inc. acted as placement agents in transaction. In consideration for their services, the company has agreed to pay the underwriters a cash commission equal to 6% of the gross proceeds from the offering and that number of non-transferable compensation options as is equal to 6% of the aggregate number of units sold under the offering. Each compensation option is exercisable to acquire one share of common stock of the company at a price equal to the lowest price permitted under TSXV policies for a period of 24 months from the closing date. 

In connection with the closing of the offering, the company paid to the underwriters aggregate cash fees in the amounts of CAD 1,455,480 and CAD 1,647,672.58 and issued to the underwriters an aggregate of 25,325,428 non-transferrable compensation options representing 6% of the gross proceeds of the offering, other than the gross proceeds raised from certain sales pursuant to a president’s list and 3% of the gross proceeds raised from president’s list sales. Each compensation option is exercisable to acquire one common share of the company at a price of CAD 0.12 per share for a period of 24 months. The company paid ZED a cash fee of CAD 52,005 and issued  520,052 compensation options.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Bunker Hill Mining was seeking to raise capital to support the construction, start-up, and ramp-up of the Bunker Hill Zinc-Silver-Lead Mine.
Teck Resources already held a significant stake in Bunker Hill, owning approximately 23.6% of the issued and outstanding Common Shares before the deal.
Bunker Hill Mining was in a position of needing additional financing to advance its mining project.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Teck subscribed for a substantial number of Units in the USD Offering, increasing its ownership stake in Bunker Hill to approximately 32.6% on a non-diluted basis.

Teck's investment was made for investment purposes, indicating a strategic interest in Bunker Hill's future and potential growth.

The deal provided essential funding for advancing its mining project, which aligns with resource acquisition and exploration objectives.
Secondary Driver Justification: Teck Resources increased its ownership stake in Bunker Hill from approximately 23.6% to 32.6% on a non-diluted basis through this deal. This significant investment not only strengthens Teck's position but also expands Bunker Hill's market presence, reflecting a strategic interest in enhancing market share.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Finance: The text details a significant financial issuance conducted by Bunker Hill Mining Corp, including details about capital raised for a mining project, indicating the integration of environmental and governance factors into financial decisions. 2. Evidence: The mention of raising approximately C$51.85 million for supporting mining operations underlines the intent to secure funds for sustainable business practices related to mining, which directly aligns with the definition of sustainable finance.

Immediately prior to the date hereof, Teck beneficially owned, directly or indirectly, or exercised control or direction over, 23,784,723 common shares and warrants to purchase an additional 2,951,389 common shares, representing approximately 6.6% of the issued and outstanding common shares on a non-diluted basis and approximately 7.4% on a partially diluted basis. Upon closing of the private placement, Teck now beneficially owns, directly or indirectly, or exercises control or direction over 219,079,378 common shares and warrants to purchase an additional 100,598,716 common shares, representing approximately 23.9% of the issued and outstanding common shares on a non-diluted basis and, assuming the exercise of all warrants now held by Teck, approximately 31.4% on a partially diluted basis. In connection with the brokered offering, the corporation grants the agents an option, exercisable in whole or in part by the lead agent, on behalf of itself and the other agents, at any time for a period of up to 48 hours prior to the closing date, to arrange for the purchase from the corporation of up to an additional 9,312,928 units for gross proceeds of CAD 1,396,939.2. The purchase price for each additional unit shall be equal to the issue price. In consideration of the agents’ services to be rendered in connection with the offering, including assisting in preparing documentation relating to the offered securities and distributing the offered securities, directly and through other investment dealers and brokers, the corporation shall pay to the agents a cash fee equal to 6% of the aggregate purchase price of offered securities sold under the offering to subscribers and 3% of the aggregate purchase price of offered securities sold under the offering to president’s list purchasers. It includes participation from 36 investors under Regulation D.

Bunker Hill Mining Corp. announced a private placement on March 5, 2025. The company will issue up to 190,476,190 units at a price of CAD 0.150885 per unit for gross proceeds of CAD 28,740,000 under brokered placement and up to 380,952,381 units at a price of CAD 0.150885 per unit for gross proceeds of CAD 57,480,000. the fees and expenses payable in connection with the Brokered Offering, will be contained in an agency agreement to be entered into by the Company and the Agents prior to the closing of the Brokered Offering.

On March 25, 2025 the company amended the terms of the transaction and now will issue units for minimum gross proceeds of CAD 14,370,000 and maximum gross proceeds of CAD 21,555,000 under brokered offering and will issue units for gross proceeds of CAD 43,110,000 under non-brokered offering. The closing of the Private Placements remain subject to approval from the TSX Venture Exchange. The transaction is subject to shareholder approval. The Company expects that Teck will beneficially own, directly or indirectly, or exercise control or direction over, 309,499,008 Common Shares and warrants to purchase an additional 145,808,532 Common Shares, representing approximately 29.6% of the Company’s then issued and outstanding Common Shares on a non-diluted basis and approximately 38.2% on a partially diluted basis.

On May 16, 2025, the company announced that it intends to issue up to 62,086,187 units at an issue price of CAD 0.150885 per unit for gross proceeds of CAD 9,367,874.325495 (brokered offering). In connection with the Brokered Offering, company  intends to grant the Agents an option, exercisable by the Agents in whole or in part at any time up to 48 hours before the closing date of the Brokered Offering, to increase the size of the Brokered Offering by up to 9,312,928 additional Units at a price of $0.1051 (CAD 0.15) per additional Unit for additional aggregate gross proceeds of up to approximately US$977,857 (CAD 1,405,181.14128) (the “Agents’ Option”). As previously announced, in a concurrent non-brokered private placement (the “Non-Brokered Offering” and, together with the Brokered Offering, the “Offerings”), Teck has agreed to contribute $2.00 for every $1.00 raised in the Brokered Offering and pursuant to the Debt Settlements and Equity Payment Agreement (each as defined herein and further described below), for a lead order of up to 209,523,809 Units at the Offering Price for aggregate consideration of up to approximately $22,000,000 (assuming a maximum aggregate funding amount of $11,000,000 comprised of proceeds from the Brokered Offering, Debt Settlements and Equity Payment Agreement (the “Funding Amount”). Each Unit issued under the Offerings will consist of one share of common stock and one-half of one Common Share purchase warrant ), with each whole Warrant exercisable for one additional Common Share at a price of CAD 0.25 per Warrant Share for a period of three years following the date of issuance. Sprott Streaming is independently making such Common Shares available to such investors in support of the Brokered Offering on a private basis and in recognition of current equity market conditions, and will not receive any direct or indirect consideration or compensation for doing so. 

BMO Nesbitt Burns Inc. acted as a lead agent and CIBC World Markets, Inc. and Red Cloud Securities Inc. acted as placement agents and bookrunners to the company. Blakes, Cassels & Graydon LLP acted as a legal counsel to the company. Computershare Trust Company of Canada acted as a transfer agent to the company.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Bunker Hill Mining was in the process of raising funds through a private placement of units.
Teck Resources already owned approximately 23.6% of Bunker Hill's issued and outstanding common shares before the deal.
Bunker Hill was seeking to support the construction, start-up, and ramp-up of the Bunker Hill Zinc-Silver-Lead Mine.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Teck subscribed for a significant number of units in the USD Offering, increasing its ownership stake in Bunker Hill.
The deal provided Bunker Hill with necessary capital to advance its mining project.
Teck's investment was made for investment purposes, indicating confidence in Bunker Hill's future prospects.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal provided Bunker Hill with necessary capital to advance its mining project, specifically the construction, start-up, and ramp-up of the Bunker Hill Zinc-Silver-Lead Mine. This indicates a clear intent to acquire and explore resources essential for the company's operations and growth in the mining sector.

This investment reflects Teck's confidence in Bunker Hill's future prospects and aligns with its broader strategy to strengthen its position in the market.
rationale: 1. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: AbraSilver Resource Corp. operates as an exploration company in Argentina, Chile, and Canada. It primarily explores for silver, gold, copper, and other metals. The company holds interest in the Diablillos property located in the Salta province of northwestern Argentina. It also has an option agreement to acquire a 100% interest in the La Coipita project in San Juan province, Argentina. The company was formerly known as AbraPlata Resource Corp. and changed its name to AbraSilver Resource Corp. in March 2021. AbraSilver Resource Corp. was incorporated in 1993 and is headquartered in Toronto, Canada.
Deal Resolution: On December 19, 2024, AbraSilver Resource Corp. closed the transaction.
Transaction Comments: AbraSilver Resource Corp. announced that it has completed a non-brokered private placement that it has issued 408,163 common shares of the Company at an issue price of CAD 3.47 per share for the gross proceeds of up to CAD 1,416,325.61 on December 19, 2024. Teck Resources Limited , the sole subscriber under the Private Placement, subscribed for Shares in connection with the option and joint venture agreement entered into among the Company and a subsidiary of Teck, as previously announced by the Company on January 22, 2024. The Shares issued pursuant to the Private Placement are subject to a statutory hold period of four months and one day.
Pre_Deal_Situation: AbraSilver Resource Corporation was viewed as a prime vehicle to capitalize on a structural bull market in precious metals, particularly silver.
Silver was considered undervalued relative to gold and historic inflation-adjusted highs.
The company operated in Argentina, which had improved its mining investment climate under the administration of Javier Milei.
AbraSilver's flagship asset, the Diablillos project, was transitioning from the engineering phase toward development, with a Definitive Feasibility Study (DFS) expected in Q2 2026.
The Diablillos project had a projected 13-year mine life with significant silver and gold production.
AbraSilver had a secondary asset, the La Coipita copper-gold project, which involved collaboration with Teck for exploration funding.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: The collaboration with Teck allowed AbraSilver to retain upside potential without capital outlay, as Teck would fund exploration costs.
Teck's involvement provided additional financial and operational support to advance the La Coipita project.
The strategic partnership aimed to leverage Teck's expertise in mining and exploration to enhance the value of AbraSilver's assets.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal was driven by a strategic partnership with Teck, which provided AbraSilver with financial and operational support to advance its projects. This collaboration allowed AbraSilver to retain upside potential without incurring capital costs, indicating a clear intent to leverage Teck's expertise in mining and exploration.
Secondary Driver Justification: The partnership with Teck specifically aimed to enhance the value of AbraSilver's assets, particularly the La Coipita project, through exploration funding. This indicates a focus on resource acquisition and exploration, as Teck's involvement was crucial for advancing the exploration efforts without direct financial burden on AbraSilver.
rationale:
1. Sustainable Operations: The classification is based on AbraSilver Resource Corp.'s focus on the mining sector, specifically its efforts to transition the Diablillos project from the engineering phase to development, which involves optimizing operational systems for resource extraction. 
2. Logic: The mention of a Definitive Feasibility Study (DFS) indicates efforts toward improving operational efficiency and sustainability within mining practices by assessing the project's environmental impacts and operational viability. 
3. Evidence: Phrases such as 'transitioning from the engineering phase toward development' and 'expected in Q2 2026' highlight a commitment to structured, sustainable progress in their operations.

The transaction included participation from Dennis Moore for 500,000 units; and Teck Resources Limited. The company paid a total of CAD 32,250 in finders' fees in connection with the private placement as permitted by applicable securities laws and the rules of the TSX-V. The company raised CAD 200,000 pursuant to Regulation D in the transaction from three investors.
Transaction Comments:
Fremont Gold Ltd. announced a non-brokered private placement of up to 10,000,000 units at a price of CAD 0.10 per unit for gross proceeds of up to CAD 1,000,000 on July 5, 2024. Each unit will be comprised of one common share and one half of one share purchase warrant. Each whole share purchase warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one share at a price of CAD 0.17 for a period of 18 months following the close of the placement. The transaction included participation from lead investor Dennis Moore, President & CEO and majority shareholder of the company; and certain directors and/or officers of the company, existing shareholders and insiders. The placement is subject to regulatory approval and all securities issued pursuant to the placement are subject to a four-month hold period under applicable Canadian securities laws. The company may pay finders' fees in connection with the placement, as permitted by applicable securities laws and the rules of the TSX Venture Exchange. The finders' fee will consist of a commission equal to 6% of proceeds raised, payable in cash.

On July 9, 2024, the company announced that it will now issue up to 17,500,000 units at a price of CAD 0.10 per unit for gross proceeds of up to CAD 1,750,000.

Haywood Securities Inc.; Ventum Financial Corp.; Research Capital Corporation and Joel Sutherland acted as placement agent to the company.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Hayasa Metals was conducting exploration at the Vardenis Copper-Gold Project in Armenia.
The company had completed a 2025 drill program that confirmed the presence of a large porphyry system.
Hayasa had previously entered into an option agreement to acquire up to a 100% interest in Mendia Resources LLC, which owns the Vardenis project.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Teck Resources was granted the option to acquire up to an 80% interest in Mendia Resources LLC, indicating a strategic partnership.
Teck's geological expertise was deemed invaluable for planning and executing the drilling campaign.
The deal allows Hayasa to leverage Teck's resources and experience in further exploration and development of the Vardenis project.

This partnership is crucial as it allows Hayasa to leverage Teck's geological expertise and resources for the exploration and development of the Vardenis project, enhancing their operational capabilities in the region.
Secondary Driver Justification: The involvement of Teck Resources, known for its geological expertise, indicates a focus on technology acquisition and innovation. Their expertise is deemed invaluable for planning and executing the drilling campaign, which suggests that Hayasa is looking to enhance its technological capabilities in mineral exploration through this partnership.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Business Building: The text describes Hayasa Metals Inc., which is focused on the exploration and evaluation of mineral properties, emphasizing the strategic partnership with Teck Resources for the Vardenis copper-gold project. This indicates an intention to develop sustainable practices in mineral extraction and project management. **2. Logic: The text outlines a deal that allows Hayasa to leverage Teck’s geological expertise for further exploration and project development, highlighting a collaborative effort in the sustainable mining sector, which is essential for building sustainable business models. **3. Evidence: The mention of 'strategic partnership' and the focus on resource leveraging for exploration and development are key indicators of sustainable business building, aimed at capturing new profit pools while maintaining environmentally responsible practices.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Hannan Metals Ltd., a junior mineral exploration company, engages in the acquisition and exploration of mineral properties in Canada, Ireland, and Peru. Deal Resolution: On June 27, 2024, Hannan Metals Ltd. closed the transaction. The company has issued an aggregate of 10,593,513 units at a price of CAD 0.35 per unit for gross proceeds of CAD 3,707,729.55. Each warrant entitles the holder to purchase one additional share of the company at an exercise price of CAD 0.50 for a period of two years expiring on June 27, 2026. Teck Resources Limited exercised its right to maintain its 9.84% partially diluted ownership interest in the company and subscribed for 714,286 units for gross proceeds to Hannan of CAD 250,000. No finder's fees or commissions are payable on the Offering. Directors of the company participated in the Offering and purchased an aggregate of 258,571 Units. All securities issued in connection with the Offering are subject to a statutory four-month hold period, expiring on October 28, 2024. The Offering is subject to final approval of the TSX Venture Exchange. The Private Placement was unanimously approved by the board of directors of the company, with interested directors having declared their interest and abstaining from voting on their respective interest therein. The company announced that it has raised CAD 1,585,587.95 ($1,163,397) from 23 investors pursuant to regulation D.
Transaction Comments:
Hannan Metals Ltd. announced a non-brokered private placement of up to 7,142,857 units at a price of CAD 0.35 per unit for the gross proceeds of CAD 2,500,000 on June 4, 2024. Each unit comprises one common share and one-half of one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant entitles the holder to purchase one additional share of the company at an exercise price of CAD 0.50 for a period of two years from closing of the offering. Finder's fees may be payable on a portion of the offering. Certain insiders of the company will participate in the offering. Each unit comprises one common share and one-half of one common share purchase warrant. Each whole warrant entitles the holder to purchase one additional share of the company at an exercise price of CAD 0.5 for a period of two years from closing of the offering. Certain insiders of the company will participate in the offering. Finder's fees may be payable on a portion of the offering. All securities to be issued pursuant to the offering will be subject to a four-month hold period under applicable securities laws in Canada. The offering is subject to certain conditions customary for transactions of this nature, including, but not limited to, the receipt of all necessary approvals, including the approval of the exchange. The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about June 26, 2024. The transaction has been oversubscribed.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Hannan Metals was focused on resource exploration and was seen as an undervalued company with high growth potential.
Teck Resources faced challenges with labor availability and issues with autonomous systems affecting its copper production.
The copper market was experiencing volatility due to various factors, including supply constraints and economic challenges in China.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Teck sought to enhance its resource portfolio amidst declining production forecasts and operational challenges.
The deal provided Hannan Metals with an opportunity to leverage Teck's resources and expertise for growth.
The strategic partnership aimed to capitalize on the rising demand for copper driven by energy transition sectors.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal was primarily driven by Teck's need to enhance its resource portfolio in light of declining production forecasts and operational challenges. Hannan Metals, being an undervalued company with high growth potential, presents a valuable opportunity for resource acquisition and exploration, particularly in the copper sector, which is experiencing rising demand.
Secondary Driver Justification: The strategic partnership between Teck and Hannan Metals is aimed at leveraging Teck's resources and expertise for growth. This collaboration is particularly relevant given the context of the energy transition sectors driving demand for copper, indicating a mutual benefit in forming a partnership to navigate market challenges.
rationale: 1. Energy Transition: The text highlights a strategic partnership aimed at capitalizing on the rising demand for copper driven by energy transition sectors. This indicates a focus on transitioning to renewable energy sources, which aligns with the class definition of mastering the shift from fossil-based energy systems. 2. Evidence: The rationale is supported by phrases in the text such as 'to enhance its resource portfolio' and 'rising demand for copper driven by energy transition sectors,' which specifically connect the company's activities with the broader energy transition initiative.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Coppernico Metals Inc. engages in the acquisition and exploration of mineral properties in South America. The company explores for copper and gold deposits. Its flagship property is the Sombrero project covering an area of approximately 102,300 hectares located in southern Peru. The company was formerly known as Sombrero Resources Inc. and changed its name to Coppernico Metals Inc. in December 2021. Coppernico Metals Inc. was incorporated in 2020 and is based in Vancouver, Canada.
Deal Resolution:
On May 16, 2024, Coppernico Metals Inc. closed the transaction. The company has now issued 38,750,580 units at a price of CAD 0.50 per unit for the gross proceeds of CAD 19,375,290 of which Teck Resources Limited acquired 17,546,580 Units, 9,870,000 Units were issued as part of the non-brokered portion of the Offering and 11,334,000 Units were sold by a syndicate of agents. Each Warrant is exercisable to acquire an additional Share at a price of $0.75 until May 16, 2026. Teck now owns a 9.9% interest in the current outstanding Shares and also acquired 8,773,290 Warrants and 17,546,580 Special Warrants included in the Units. If the Warrants are exercised, Teck’s ownership percentage will increase to a maximum of 14.15% of outstanding Shares if other dilutive securities remained unexercised at the time. All of the securities issued pursuant to the Offering are subject to a four-month hold period in accordance with applicable Canadian securities laws. In addition, the Shares and Warrants issued to Teck are subject to a twelve-month restriction on sale. After the twelve-month period, Teck shall have the right to sell its Shares and Warrants in excess of 2% of the outstanding Shares, subject to the Company retaining a right to designate an alternative purchasers within ten business days of receipt of the notice of intention to sell from Teck.

The company has received CAD 1,720,899($1,262,480) pursuant to Regulation D and included participation from 43 investors.
Headquarters - Country/Region [Buyers/In: Minerco, Inc. (United States); Teck Resources Limited (TSX:TECK.B) (Canada)
Transaction Comments:
Coppernico Metals Inc. announced that it has signed a non-binding term sheet on best efforts basis for issuance of 30,000,000 units at a price of CAD 0.50 per unit for gross proceeds of CAD 15,000,000 on May 2, 2024. Each unit will consist of one common share and one half of a share purchase warrant. Each warrant will be exercisable to acquire an additional share at a price of CAD 0.75 for a period of two years from completion of the offering. If the closing price of the shares on a recognized stock exchange, if and when the shares are listed, meets or exceeds CAD 1.25 for 20 out of any 30 consecutive trading days ending at least four months after the closing, the company may accelerate the expiry date of the warrants to a date that is not less than 30 days from the date when such notice is given. The transaction included participation from new investor Minerco, Inc. for an equity stake of 9.9%. The shares and warrants issued in the offering will be subject to a statutory hold period of four-months plus one day. The closing is expected to be on or about May 15, 2024 and is subject to execution of a definitive agreement with MinerCo, completing the balance of the minimum Offering and customary Closing deliveries. The offering size may be increased by up to 40% in which case the value received from MinerCo for the 9.9% investment would correspondingly increase.

3L Capital and Beacon Securities Limited acted as co-lead agents on behalf of a syndicate of agents and Canaccord Genuity Corp. and Research Capital Corporation acted as placement agents to the company. In connection with the Offering, the company paid a success fee of CAD 263,198.70 to Minvisory Corp. who acted as financial advisor to the company in relation to the Teck investment. The Company also paid a cash fee of CAD 340,020 and issued 680,040 compensation options to the Agents in relation to the brokered portion of the Offering, and paid a cash fee of CAD 87,300 and issued 174,600 Compensation Options to an additional finder in relation to the non-brokered portion of the Offering. Each Compensation Option entitles the holder thereof to subscribe for one Share at CAD 0.50 until May 16, 2026.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Coppernico Metals was actively exploring the Nioc target within its Sombrero Project in Peru.
Recent channel sampling indicated significant copper mineralization, with results showing continuous mineralization over substantial widths.
The copper sector was experiencing structural strain, with declining ore grades and increasing production timelines.
Teck Resources held a 9.9% stake in Coppernico, indicating a strategic interest in the company's developments.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: The deal allowed Teck to leverage Coppernico's promising exploration results and potential for significant copper resources.
Teck aimed to strengthen its position in the copper market amidst rising demand driven by decarbonization and electric vehicle growth.
The collaboration could enhance the development of district-scale copper skarn and porphyry systems across the Sombrero Project.
Teck's involvement could provide additional resources and expertise to advance Coppernico's drilling and exploration efforts.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal allowed Teck to leverage Coppernico's promising exploration results and potential for significant copper resources, indicating a clear intent to acquire and explore valuable mineral assets. Additionally, Teck's involvement could provide additional resources and expertise to advance Coppernico's drilling and exploration efforts, further emphasizing the focus on resource acquisition and exploration.
Secondary Driver Justification: Teck aimed to strengthen its position in the copper market amidst rising demand driven by decarbonization and electric vehicle growth, which aligns with market share expansion. By increasing its stake in Coppernico, Teck is positioning itself to capture a larger share of the growing copper market, particularly as demand increases due to industry trends.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Business Building: The rationale for classifying this text under 'Sustainable Business Building' lies in its emphasis on Coppernico Metals Inc.'s strategic partnerships and operations aimed at enhancing exploration and development of sustainable copper resources. The text describes how Coppernico's collaboration with Teck Resources is intended to leverage promising exploration results and respond to rising demand due to decarbonization initiatives, particularly with the growth of electric vehicles. This clearly illustrates the focus on creating sustainable business models in the mineral exploration sector. **2. Evidence in the text: The text states, 'The deal allowed Teck to leverage Coppernico's promising exploration results and potential for significant copper resources,' which explicitly connects the business activities to sustainability. Furthermore, the mention of 'enhance the development of district-scale copper skarn and porphyry systems' reinforces the intent to build a sustainable operation in the face of market demand.

Perseverance had attracted a strong shareholder base, including strategic shareholders like Teck Resources.

Secondary Driver Justification: Teck's involvement as a strategic shareholder signifies a partnership that aligns with both companies' interests in advancing nickel exploration and development. This collaboration enhances Perseverance's strategic positioning in the critical minerals sector, showcasing the importance of strategic partnerships in this deal.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Finance: The rationale for classifying this text under Sustainable Finance stems from the company’s activities related to the critical minerals sector, particularly how their partnerships and deals, such as with Teck Resources, suggest a focus on funding for sustainable development within the industry. **2. Logic: The text highlights the involvement of strategic shareholders and necessary funding for exploration efforts, which align with the goals of integrating environmental, social, and governance (ESG) considerations. **3. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: American Eagle Gold Corp. acquires and explores mineral properties. The company explores for gold and copper deposits in North America. Deal Resolution: On November 21, 2023, American Eagle Gold Corp. closed the transaction. The TSX Venture Exchange has accepted for filing documentation with respect to the transaction.

rationale: 1. Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Hannan Metals Ltd., a junior mineral exploration company, engages in the acquisition and exploration of mineral properties in Canada, Ireland, and Peru. The company explores for copper, gold, silver, zinc, and lead deposits. Its flagship project is the 100% owned the San Martin project located northwest of Tarapoto, Peru. The company was formerly known as Mitchell Resources Ltd. and changed its name to Hannan Metals Ltd. in January 2017. The company is based in Vancouver, Canada.
Deal Resolution: On May 9, 2023, Hannan Metals Ltd. closed the transaction. The company issued 7,044,460 units at an issue price of CAD 0.25 per unit for the gross proceeds of CAD 1,761,115. The transaction included participation from 24 placees, total existing insider involvement 180,000 (four insiders), returning investor Teck Resources Limited for 1,120,000 units for gross proceeds of CAD 280,000 and certain directors and insider investors for 180,000 units. Teck will hold 9.9% of the issued common shares and warrants on a partially diluted basis. The transaction was oversubscribed. Each Warrant entitles the holder to purchase one additional share of the company at an exercise price of CAD 0.35 for a period of three years expiring on May 9, 2026. All securities issued in connection with the offering are subject to a statutory four-month hold period, expiring on September 10, 2023. The company paid a cash commission of CAD 2,250 to a finder. The transaction is subject to final approval of the exchange. The transaction was unanimously approved by the board of directors of the company. The company received CAD 928,242 from 7 investors pursuant to Regulation D in the transaction
Transaction Comments: Hannan Metals Ltd. announced a non-brokered private placement financing of up to 5,360,000 units at a price of CAD 0.25 per unit for gross proceeds of up to CAD 1,340,000 on April 24, 2023. Each unit comprises one common share and one-half of one common share purchase warrant. Each Warrant entitles the holder to purchase one additional Share of the Company at an exercise price of CAD 0.35 for a period of three years from closing of the Offering. The Company has the right to force conversion of the Warrants, if at any time from and after the date of issuance, the weighted average closing price of the Company's common shares on the TSX Venture Exchange, equals or exceeds CAD 0.50 for 20 consecutive trading days. Finder's fees may be payable on a portion of the Offering. Certain insiders of the Company will participate in the Offering. All securities to be issued will be subject to a four-month hold period under applicable securities laws in Canada. The Offering is subject to certain conditions customary for transactions of this nature, including, but not limited to, the receipt of all necessary approvals, including the approval of the Exchange.
