Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Kodiak Copper Corp. engages in the exploration and development of base metals properties in North America. It explores for gold, copper, molybdenum, silver, and diamond deposits. The company's advanced asset is the 100% owned Man, Prime and Dillard Property covering approximately 338 square kilometers located in the prolific Quesnel Trough in southern British Columbia, Canada. It holds 100% interests in the Mohave Project which covers approximately 10.4 square kilometers located in the Arizona, the United States; and in the Kahuna Property located in the Nunavut, Canada. The company was formerly known as Dunnedin Ventures Inc. and changed its name to Kodiak Copper Corp. in April 2020. The company was incorporated in 1987 and is headquartered in Vancouver, Canada.
Deal Resolution: On April 21, 2023, Kodiak Copper Corp. closed the transaction. The company has amended the terms of the transaction. The company has issued 1,532,654 charity flow-through shares at price of CAD 1.32 for the gross proceeds of CAD 2,023,103 and 1,461,090 flow-through units at a price of CAD 0.96 for the gross proceeds CAD 1,402,646 for aggregate gross proceeds of CAD 8,402,749. Each FT Warrant issuable under the Concurrent Financing will entitle the holder to purchase one nonflow-through Common Share at an exercise price of CAD 1.10 for a period of 24 months following the closing date. Further, in the event that the Common Shares' price closes at or above CAD 1.70 on the TSX Venture Exchange for 20 consecutive trading days, the Company may, within 15 days of the occurrence of such event, deliver a notice to the holders of the FT Warrants accelerating the expiry date of the FT warrants to the date that is 30 days following such notice, and any unexercised FT warrants after such period shall automatically expire. The Common Shares, the FT warrants and the Warrant Shares are subject to a four month hold period, expiring on August 22, 2023. The Concurrent Financing remains subject to final acceptance of the TSX-V. The transaction included participation Teck Resources Limited for 592,593 units. The transaction also included participation from Chris Taylor and other insiders of the company for 301.042 flow through units The company has paid a finders fees of CAD 73,278. The transaction included participation from three insiders, 64 placees and one pro group in the transaction.
Transaction Comments:
Kodiak Copper Corp. announced a bought deal private placement of 1,500,000 charity flow-through units at a price of CAD 1.32 per charity FT unit for gross proceeds of CAD 1,980,000, that will be issued as part of a charity arrangement, each of which Charity FT Unit will consist of one common share of the company and one-half of one common share purchase warrant, and 3,700,000 common share units at a price of CAD 0.81 per unit for gross proceeds of CAD 2,997,000, with each of which common share unit will consist of one non-flow through common share and one-half of one non-flow-through common share purchase warrant for proceeds of CAD 4,977,000 as well as a non-brokered private placement of up to 833,333 charity flow-through units at CAD 1.32 for proceeds of CAD 1,099,999.56, and up to 418,498 flow-through units, each of which flow-through unit will consist of one common share and one-half of one flow-through warrant, both of which will qualify as flow-through shares at a price of CAD 0.96 for CAD 401,758.08, for aggregate gross proceeds of up to CAD 1,501,757.64 for total proceeds of CAD 6,478,757.64 on April 4, 2023. Each flow-through warrant and warrant issuable will entitle the holder to purchase one non-flow-through common share at an exercise price of CAD 1.10 for a period of 24 months following the closing date. The transaction may include participation from certain insiders and shareholders of the company. The transaction is expected to close on or about April 14, 2023, or such other date as the Company and the Underwriter may agree and is subject to certain conditions including, but not limited to, the receipt of all necessary regulatory and other approvals including the acceptance of the TSX-V. The securities issued pursuant to non-brokered private placement will be subject to a hold period of four months and one day pursuant to applicable Canadian securities laws. 

On April 6, 2023, the company has amended the terms of the transaction. The company now issuing 1,200,000 charity flow-through units for the gross proceeds of CAD 1,584,000 and 1,000,000 flow-through units for the gross proceeds of CAD 960,000.

On April 14, 2023, the company extended the period of offering. The transaction is now expected to close on April 21, 2023. Also, as part of transaction, the company will pay a cash commission of CAD 298,050, which is equal to 6 per cent of the gross proceeds of the offering. Certain insiders and shareholders of the company are expected to participate in the concurrent financing.

Cormark Securities Inc. acted as lead underwriter and sole bookrunner to the company in the transaction. Canaccord Genuity Corp. acted as placement agent to the company in the transaction. the company has paid CAD 298.050 agent fees to the Cormark Securities Inc.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Copper demand was increasing due to global electrification and renewable energy trends.
Investors were cautious about buying futures contracts and preferred publicly traded producers.
Copper prices had decreased from pandemic highs but remained higher than pre-pandemic levels.
There was a limited number of new copper discoveries and declining ore grades, leading to potential supply constraints.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: The deal allows Kodiak Copper to leverage Teck's established operations and expertise.
It positions Kodiak to benefit from rising global demand for copper, particularly in EVs and renewable energy.
The acquisition enhances Kodiak's production capacity and reduces geopolitical risks associated with mining.
Teck's strong balance sheet and cash flow generation can support Kodiak's growth and operational efficiency.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal positions Kodiak Copper to benefit from rising global demand for copper, particularly in electric vehicles (EVs) and renewable energy, which are high-growth sectors. This aligns with the increasing copper demand driven by global electrification trends, indicating a strategic focus on growth exposure in a thematic market.
Secondary Driver Justification: The partnership with Teck Resources allows Kodiak to leverage Teck's established operations and expertise, which can enhance operational efficiency. Additionally, Teck's strong balance sheet and cash flow generation can support Kodiak's growth, further improving its operational capabilities.
rationale: 1. Energy Transition: The rationale for this classification stems from the text indicating rising global demand for copper, particularly in relation to electric vehicles (EVs) and renewable energy. This aligns with the definition of Energy Transition, which involves the global shift from fossil-based energy production to renewable sources. 2. Evidence: The text discusses how the increasing demand for copper is influenced by trends in global electrification and renewable energy, positioning Kodiak Copper to leverage these trends, thereby supporting the transition to a more sustainable energy future.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: CR Property comprises 19 contiguous mineral claims. The asset is located in Canada.
Deal Resolution: Sun Summit Minerals Corp. (TSXV:SMN) completed the acquisition of CR Property from Teck Resources Limited (TSX:TECK.B) on June 13, 2023.

The CR property is located adjacent to Sun Summit's existing Buck property, allowing for more efficient exploration and development.

Additionally, the CR property is located adjacent to Sun Summit's existing Buck property, enhancing their overall land footprint in the region.
Secondary Driver Justification: The proximity of the CR property to Sun Summit's Buck Project allows for more efficient exploration and development. rationale: 1. Sustainable Operations: The text describes Sun Summit Minerals Corp. acquiring the CR Property to enhance its exploration activities, which aligns with sustainable operational practices focused on optimizing exploration efforts. ** 2. Logic: The intent behind this acquisition is to improve efficiency in exploration and to build upon previous findings, which demonstrates a commitment to responsible resource management. ** 3. Evidence: The text states that the acquisition "expanded Sun Summit's land position by more than 20%, increasing its exploration potential," indicating a strategic decision aimed at sustainable development within the mining sector.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Fort Hills Energy Limited Partnership develops, explores, and produces oil sands mines. The company is based in Calgary, Canada. Fort Hills Energy Limited Partnership operates as a subsidiary of Suncor Energy Inc.
Deal Resolution: Total E&P Canada Ltd. completed the acquisition of an additional 6.65% stake in Fort Hills Energy Limited Partnership from Teck Resources Limited (TSX:TECK.B) on February 5, 2023. J.P. Morgan Securities Canada Inc. acted as financial advisor to Teck Resources Limited in the transaction.
Primary Industry [Target/Issuer]: Oil and Gas Exploration and Production
Transaction Comments: Total E&P Canada Ltd. entered into an agreement to acquire an additional 6.65% stake in Fort Hills Energy Limited Partnership from Teck Resources Limited (TSX:TECK.B) for approximately CAD 310 million on January 27, 2023. Total E&P Canada Ltd. announces that it has exercised its preemption right to acquire an additional 6.65% interest in the Fort Hills Energy Limited Partnership and associated sales and logistics agreements from Teck Resources Limited. Total E&P Canada Ltd. held a working interest of 24.58% in the Fort Hills project, and after the transaction it will hold 31.23%. Total E&P Canada Ltd. also holds a 50% working interest in the Surmont project located in the region. TotalEnergies SE announced in September 2022 its intention to exit Canadian oil sands by spinning off TotalEnergies EP Canada in 2023. Through the acquisition of an additional interest in Fort Hills, TotalEnergies EP Canada is building the future for the spin-off entity in an asset with long-term growth potential. The spin-off is planned to be submitted to vote at TotalEnergies' annual Shareholders' Meeting in May 2023. Vince Mercier, Anthony Spadaro, Matthew Milne-Smith, Charles Tingley, Marie-Emmanuelle Vaillancourt and Dan Pearlman of Davies Ward Phillips & Vineberg LLP acted as legal advisor to Total E&P Canada Ltd.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Teck Resources was involved in the steelmaking coal business and considering a spin-off of this segment.
Investors had expressed concerns about the steelmaking coal business.
Teck held a 21.3% interest in the Fort Hills Energy Limited Partnership.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Teck completed the sale to focus on its core business units, which include copper, zinc, and steelmaking coal.
The sale generated approximately $1 billion in cash proceeds for Teck.
Teck did not anticipate any tax payable on the disposition, enhancing the financial benefit of the sale.
Primary Driver Justification: The deal occurred as Teck Resources was considering a spin-off of its steelmaking coal business and aimed to focus on its core business units. By selling its stake in Fort Hills Energy, Teck is restructuring its portfolio to consolidate its operations around more profitable segments, which aligns with market consolidation strategies.
Secondary Driver Justification: The sale generated approximately $1 billion in cash proceeds for Teck, which indicates a financial strategy aimed at reducing costs associated with maintaining a non-core investment. Additionally, the lack of anticipated tax payable on the disposition further enhances the financial efficiency of this transaction.
rationale: 1. Energy Transition: The text discusses Fort Hills Energy Limited Partnership, which is involved in oil sands production, traditionally tied to fossil fuels. The context of Teck Resources selling its stake reflects a shift in focus that aligns with energy transition trends—from fossil-based energy systems to perhaps more sustainable opportunities in the future. 2. Logic: The deal to sell a stake in a fossil fuel venture aligns with the broader narrative of energy transition, indicative of companies looking to navigate shifts towards renewable energy sources. 3. Evidence: The mention of Teck focusing on core business units like copper and zinc implies a move away from fossil-fuel dependency, which supports the classification under Energy Transition.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Operator of steelmaking coal properties.
Deal Resolution:
12 Jun 23 - Glencore plc, the Switzerland based integrated commodities producer and marketer, has made a proposal to acquire the steelmaking coal business from Teck Resources Ltd, the Canada based diversified natural resources company, focused on coal, natural gas, copper, zinc, gold, silver and niobium mining and development.

The consideration is expected to be $8.2bn as quoted in The Globe and Mail.

17 Aug 23 - JSW Steel Ltd, the listed India based integrated steel producer, is reported to be bidding for the business from Teck.

14 Nov 23 - Glencore agreed to acquire the steelmaking coal business, which is Elk Valley Resources Ltd (EVR), for $6.93bn.

Concurrently, Nippon Steel Corp, the listed Japanese integrated steel manufacturer, will exchange its 2.5% interest in Elkview Operations with $1.3bn cash and $0.4bn cashflow for 20% stake in EVR; whereas POSCO, the listed South Korean counterpart, will exchange its 2.5% interest in Elkview Operations and its 20% interest and 20% interest in the Greenhills joint venture, for 3% stake in EVR.

EVR is valued at an enterprise value of $9bn.

The transaction is subject to mandatory regulatory approvals, being Investment Canada Act (“ICA”) and competition approvals.  The transaction is expected to close in Q3 2024.

4 July 24 - The transaction has received regulatory approval after a successful review under the Investment Canada Act.

11 Jul 24 - Deal completed.
Transaction Comments: Glencore plc acquired a 0.77% stake in Elk Valley Resources Ltd (77%) on November 14, 2023.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Lincoln County was attempting to weaken water quality standards related to selenium at Lake Koocanusa.
The Montana Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ) had denied Lincoln County's petition to roll back existing water quality criteria.
Environmental groups, including the Montana Environmental Information Center (MEIC), were actively opposing Lincoln County's efforts and seeking transparency.
There was a history of contamination from Canadian coal mining operations affecting the watershed.
Local elected leaders expressed concerns that existing selenium standards could impede economic development in the region.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Glencore, through Elk Valley Resources (EVR), sought to challenge the existing water quality standards that they viewed as restrictive to their mining operations.
The deal was likely aimed at aligning Lincoln County's regulatory stance with the interests of EVR to facilitate future mining activities.
There was a perceived need for cooperation between Lincoln County and EVR to address regulatory challenges and promote economic growth in the region.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: Operator of steelmaking coal properties.
Deal Resolution:
Glencore plc, the listed Switzerland based integrated commodities producer and marketer, agreed to acquire the steelmaking coal business of Teck Resources Ltd, the listed Canadian mining group, which is Elk Valley Resources Ltd (EVR), for $6.93bn.

Concurrently, Nippon Steel Corp, the listed Japanese integrated steel manufacturer, will exchange its 2.5% interest in Elkview Operations with $1.34bn cash and $0.4bn cashflow of EVR for 20% stake in EVR; whereas POSCO, the listed South Korean counterpart, will exchange its 2.5% interest in Elkview Operations and its 20% interest and 20% interest in the Greenhills joint venture, for 3% stake in EVR.

EVR is valued at an enterprise value of $9bn. The 20% interest in EVR is $1.8bn.

The transaction is subject to mandatory regulatory approvals, being Investment Canada Act (“ICA”) and competition approvals.  The transaction is expected to close in Q3 2024.

Business Description [Target/Issuer]: The Magnum Ice Cream Company N.V. engages in ice cream business. The Magnum Ice Cream Company N.V. is based in Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Netherlands.
Deal Resolution: Unilever PLC (LSE:ULVR) completed the 80.1%  spin-off its Ice Cream business of Unilever PLC on December 6, 2025.
Target/Issuer: The Magnum Ice Cream Company N.V. (ENXTAM:MICC)
Transaction Comments:
Unilever PLC (LSE:ULVR) agreed to spin-off its Ice Cream business of Unilever PLC on March 19, 2024. Ice Cream will be separated by way of demerger, through listing of the business in Amsterdam, London, and New York. TMICC and Unilever entered into the Demerger Agreement for the implementation of the Demerger on October 1, 2025. As of March 19, 2025, Unilever announced that its Ice Cream business will be known as ‘The Magnum Ice Cream Company’. For every five Unilever shares held, shareholders will receive one share in The Magnum Ice Cream Company. Qualifying ADS Holders will hold approximately 80.1 percent of the issued share capital of TMICC and the Unilever Group will hold approximately 19.9 percent. Simultaneous to this demerger of Magnum, HUL is also spinning off Kwality Walls (India) Limited.

The Magnum Ice Cream Company (TMICC) will be led by Peter ter Kulve as CEO and Abhijit Bhattacharya as CFO, and Jean-Francois van Boxmeer has been appointed as Chair Designate for the separated Ice Cream business.

The spinoff is subject to approvals from the board and shareholders of Unilever PLC, regulatory board and Financial Conduct Authority. The separation of the Ice Cream business is expected to be completed by the end of 2025. The Demerger Record Time is expected on November 7, 2025. The Demerger is expected to complete on Saturday, November 8, 2025, with admission of TMICC shares to listing and trading anticipated to take place on November 10, 2025. European consumer products giant Unilever said that it has revised the timetable for the spin-off of The Magnum Ice Cream Company as a result of the U.S. federal government shutdown, but said it remains confident of implementing it in 2025. The HUL board fixed December 5, 2025, as the record date to determine the eligible shareholders of the company entitled to receive equity shares of KWIL. Demerger is expected to take place on December 6, 2025.

Linklaters LLP acted as legal advisor and Computershare Investor Services PLC acted as registrar to Unilever PLC.
Pre_Deal_Situation: Magnum Ice Cream Company was recently spun off from Unilever.
The company experienced a significant stock price drop of approximately 18% due to a substantial decline in profits over the past year.
Costs associated with the separation negatively impacted the net results of Magnum Ice Cream Company.
Why_The_Deal_Happened: Unilever aimed to streamline its operations by divesting non-core brands.
The spin-off was intended to allow Magnum Ice Cream Company to focus on its brand and growth strategies independently.
The deal was part of a broader trend of consolidation in the consumer goods sector.
Master Target: The Magnum Ice Cream Company N.V. (ENXTAM:MICC)
Primary Driver Justification: The deal reflects a broader trend of consolidation in the consumer goods sector, indicating that Unilever's divestment of its ice cream business is part of a strategic move to restructure its portfolio. This aligns with the intent to streamline operations by divesting non-core brands, which is a key aspect of market consolidation.
Secondary Driver Justification: The spin-off allows Magnum Ice Cream Company to focus on its brand and growth strategies independently, which suggests a diversification of its portfolio. By separating from Unilever, Magnum can pursue its unique growth opportunities without the constraints of being part of a larger conglomerate.
rationale: 1. Sustainable Business Building: The rationale for classifying this text under 'Sustainable Business Building' lies in the context of the spin-off of Magnum Ice Cream Company from Unilever, which was aimed at allowing the brand to focus on independent growth strategies. **2. Logic & Evidence: The text highlights Unilever's intention to streamline operations and divest non-core brands, indicating a strategic approach towards creating a business model that is more sustainable and focused on growth opportunities specific to Magnum. This aligns with the class definition centered on sustainable business development and capturing profit pools, especially post-spin-off ensuring strategic independence for growth.
